chiang_mai.name: Chiang Mai
chiang_mai.tagline: Ancient Moat City in the Mountain North

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Night Bazaar
attractions.description: Bustling night market featuring street food vendors & local crafts, plus souvenirs.

attractions.name: The Castle - Ghost House
attractions.description: This atmospheric haunted house attraction in Chiang Mai's old city delivers theatrical scares through darkened corridors filled with costumed actors and elaborate set designs. The intimate scale and creative use of traditional Thai ghost folklore makes for a uniquely local twist on the horror experience.
attractions.local_name: ปราสาทผีสิง
attractions.tip: Visit on weekday evenings when crowds are smaller for more personalized scares. The actors speak limited English, so going with Thai-speaking friends enhances the experience.

attractions.name: Muang On Cave
attractions.description: Hillside cave network with stalactites & stalagmites, plus small shrines & Buddha statues.

attractions.name: The Big Old Tree
attractions.description: This ancient tree stands as a living monument within Chiang Mai's old city walls, its massive trunk and sprawling canopy providing shade where locals gather and pray. The tree's reputation as a sacred site draws both photographers seeking that perfect shot and visitors wanting to experience a tranquil moment of local spiritual life.
attractions.local_name: ต้นไม้ใหญ่เก่าแก่
attractions.tip: Visit early morning around 6-7am when locals make offerings at the base—you'll see the tree at its most peaceful and authentic before tour groups arrive.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Gate
attractions.description: This centuries-old brick gate stands as one of five remaining entrances to Chiang Mai's walled old city, with its weathered red-brown laterite stones bearing witness to the ancient Lanna Kingdom. The gate's open archway frames perfect views of the moat and makes for excellent golden hour photography as locals pass through on motorbikes and tuk-tuks.
attractions.local_name: ประตูเชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Visit at sunrise when monks in saffron robes walk past for alms, creating authentic photo opportunities without the crowds that gather later in the day.

attractions.name: Tha Phae Walking Street
attractions.description: Each Sunday afternoon until midnight this street becomes a gallery for local arts & crafts.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Gate Market
attractions.description: Bustling open-air market with a wide range of food stalls serving street eats, sweets & produce.

attractions.name: The Moat House
attractions.description: This charming colonial-style building sits along Chiang Mai's ancient moat, offering a picturesque blend of European architecture against traditional Northern Thai surroundings. The photogenic facade and waterside location create perfect frames for capturing the old city's serene atmosphere.
attractions.local_name: เดอะ โมท เฮาส์
attractions.tip: Visit during golden hour when the low sun illuminates the cream-colored facade and casts beautiful reflections on the moat water for the best photography.

attractions.name: MOAT BAR
attractions.description: This trendy rooftop bar perched along Chiang Mai's ancient moat offers creative cocktails and unobstructed views of the old city walls at sunset. The relaxed atmosphere and photogenic setting make it perfect for unwinding after exploring the temples.
attractions.local_name: โมทบาร์
attractions.tip: Arrive around 5:30 PM to secure the best corner seats before the golden hour rush, when the moat reflects the warm sunset light beautifully.

attractions.name: Nun‘s Restaurant
attractions.description: Run by Buddhist nuns in saffron robes, this vegetarian restaurant in the Old City serves wholesome Thai dishes with proceeds supporting their temple community. The peaceful atmosphere and authentic home-style cooking prepared with mindful intention offer a window into monastic life rarely experienced by visitors.
attractions.local_name: ร้านอาหารแม่ชี
attractions.tip: Arrive before noon when the daily specials are freshly prepared and the selection is fullest, as popular dishes sell out by mid-afternoon.

attractions.name: Huen Phen
attractions.description: This traditional Northern Thai restaurant serves authentic Lanna cuisine in a charming wooden house setting within the old city walls. The khao soi and nam prik noom here represent true Chiang Mai flavors passed down through generations.
attractions.local_name: เฮือนเพ็ญ
attractions.tip: Visit during lunch for the casual daytime menu, or come after 5pm when they open the atmospheric evening dining room with traditional decor and local specialties.

attractions.name: The Old City Inn
attractions.description: This charming heritage guesthouse in the heart of Chiang Mai's old quarter showcases traditional Lanna architecture with intricately carved wooden details and a tranquil courtyard garden. The photogenic setting captures the essence of northern Thai design, making it a favorite spot for travelers seeking authentic atmosphere.
attractions.local_name: ดิ โอลด์ ซิตี้ อินน์
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon when the wooden facades glow in warm light, creating the best photo conditions without harsh shadows or crowds.

attractions.name: Airniversary Chiang Mai
attractions.description: This Chiang Mai studio specializes in dreamy indoor photography sessions with elaborate Korean-style sets, flowing fabrics, and artistic lighting designed for social media content. Perfect for travelers wanting professional portrait photos without needing perfect weather or scouting outdoor locations.
attractions.local_name: Airniversary เชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Book your session at least 3-4 days in advance and bring multiple outfit changes to maximize variety in your photos across different themed backdrops.

attractions.name: Tha Phae Gate
attractions.description: Known as a site for many community events, this preserved city gate dates back to ancient times.

attractions.name: Si Phum Corner
attractions.description: This northwest corner of Chiang Mai's ancient moat features a distinctive brick bastion that once anchored the old city's defensive walls, now surrounded by shaded gardens and flowering trees. The peaceful waterside setting offers prime photo opportunities with the historic fortification reflected in the moat, especially during golden hour.
attractions.local_name: มุมสี่พื้น
attractions.tip: Visit early morning when local residents practice tai chi by the moat and the light hits the brick structure perfectly for photos without crowds.

attractions.name: Three Kings Monument
attractions.description: Sculpture of Kings Mengrai, Ramkamhaeng and Ngam Muang, purported founding fathers of Chiang Mai.

attractions.name: Wat Umong Suan Putthatham
attractions.description: Historic Buddhist temple complex with woods, a small lake, ancient tunnels & practicing monks.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Historical Centre
attractions.description: Simple museum offering exhibits on the history of Chiang Mai, plus an inner courtyard with a shrine.

attractions.name: Ruins of Wat E-Khang
attractions.description: These atmospheric brick ruins sit quietly among residential lanes, with crumbling chedis and moss-covered walls that whisper of Lanna kingdom's 15th-century glory. The unrestored, raw state of the temple complex offers an authentic glimpse into Chiang Mai's ancient past without the tourist crowds.
attractions.local_name: วัดอีคัง
attractions.tip: Visit in late afternoon when golden light filters through the remaining structures, creating perfect photo opportunities. The ruins are unfenced and free to explore respectfully.

attractions.name: Wat Ku Pa Dom
attractions.description: This peaceful neighborhood temple features a distinctive chedi wrapped in colorful sashes and an atmospheric wooden sala where local monks gather for evening chants. The intimate grounds offer an authentic glimpse into daily monastic life away from the tourist crowds.
attractions.local_name: วัดกู่ป่าดม
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon around 5pm when monks return from alms rounds and the temple feels most alive with local activity and prayer preparations.

attractions.name: Buak Had Public Park
attractions.description: Sizable park with a contemporary playground, picnic areas, shade trees & water features.

attractions.name: Mae Kha Canal
attractions.description: This tree-lined canal runs through the Nimman area, featuring a paved walking and cycling path flanked by cafés and local eateries. The peaceful waterway offers a relaxing escape from Chiang Mai's busy streets, perfect for an early morning jog or evening stroll.
attractions.local_name: คลองแม่ข่า
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon when locals gather for exercise and street food vendors set up along the path near the Nimman intersection.

attractions.name: MAI The Sky Bar
attractions.description: This rooftop bar on the 28th floor offers sweeping views over Chiang Mai's skyline and surrounding mountains, with creative cocktails and a sophisticated atmosphere. The golden hour transforms the entire space as the sun sets behind Doi Suthep, creating perfect photo opportunities alongside your drinks.
attractions.local_name: ใหม่ เดอะ สกาย บาร์
attractions.tip: Arrive 30 minutes before sunset to secure the best corner seats by the glass railing, and try the northern Thai-inspired cocktails that aren't on the main menu.

attractions.name: Wat Chedi Luang
attractions.description: Temple complex with a partially crumbled 15th-century chedi & a pillar believed to protect the city.

attractions.name: Wat Phra Singh Woramahawihan
attractions.description: 14th-century Buddhist temple boasting gold & copper Buddhas, murals & ancient manuscripts.

attractions.name: Wiang Kum Kam
attractions.description: Tours are available of these brick ruins of an ancient city & temple in serene surrounds.

attractions.name: Wat Muen San
attractions.description: This intimate neighborhood temple features a striking multi-tiered chedi decorated with intricate mosaic patterns and Lanna-style architecture surrounded by peaceful gardens. The photogenic combination of traditional temple design with colorful tile work and serene atmosphere makes it a refreshing alternative to Chiang Mai's busier temples.
attractions.local_name: วัดม่วนสาร
attractions.tip: Visit in late afternoon when the light hits the mosaic details on the chedi perfectly for photos, and you'll likely have the entire temple grounds to yourself.

attractions.name: Wat Chiang Man
attractions.description: 13th-century temple with a gold chedi offering ancient Buddha & elephant statues on scenic grounds.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Walking Street
attractions.description: Every Sunday evening, Ratchadamnoen Road transforms into a vibrant kilometer-long marketplace where hundreds of vendors sell handcrafted items, from intricate silver jewelry to hand-painted umbrellas, alongside local street food stalls. The authentic hill tribe crafts and direct-from-artisan prices make this one of Northern Thailand's best places to find unique souvenirs.
attractions.local_name: ถนนคนเดินเชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Arrive by 5pm to browse before crowds peak at 7pm, and bring exact change in small bills since many vendors can't break large notes.

attractions.name: Wua Lai Walking Street Saturday Market
attractions.description: Outdoor bazaar with vendors selling traditional street snacks, handmade crafts & souvenirs.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Night Market
attractions.description: Bustling, colorful Sunday night market for local produce, prepared foods, crafts & souvenirs.

attractions.name: Anusarn Market
attractions.description: Bustling traditional market with many souvenir & food stalls, plus nightly cabaret shows.

attractions.name: ถนนคนเดินเชียงใหม่
attractions.description: Every Sunday evening, Ratchadamnoen Road transforms into Chiang Mai's most vibrant street market, stretching a full kilometer with hundreds of vendors selling handmade crafts, textiles, and local art. The combination of live traditional music performances, affordable northern Thai street food, and unique handcrafted souvenirs makes this the definitive Chiang Mai cultural experience.
attractions.local_name: ถนนคนเดินเชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Arrive by 5pm to browse before crowds peak at 6:30pm. The best handmade items are near Phra Singh Temple, and vendors at the back often negotiate prices more than those at the front.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Railway Station Park
attractions.description: This green pocket park alongside the historic railway station offers shaded benches, vintage train photo opportunities, and a peaceful escape from the nearby Sunday Walking Street crowds. The old locomotives on display and railway-themed installations make it a charming spot for nostalgia and relaxed people-watching.
attractions.local_name: สวนสถานีรถไฟเชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Visit early morning when local tai chi groups practice here, or late afternoon when golden light hits the old trains perfectly for photos before heading to the Sunday market.

attractions.name: Ban Den Park
attractions.description: This residential community park features a serene lake surrounded by mature trees and walking paths that locals use for morning exercise and evening strolls. The peaceful atmosphere and photogenic wooden bridges over the water make it a relaxing escape from Chiang Mai's busier tourist areas.
attractions.local_name: บ้านเดิน พาร์ค
attractions.tip: Visit during golden hour around 5-6pm when families gather and the light creates beautiful reflections on the lake for photography.

attractions.name: Wat Suan Dok
attractions.description: Buddhist temple in tranquil surrounds with a golden dome, ornate interior & historic mausoleum.

attractions.name: Ethical elephant sanctuary chiangmai by Big Boy
attractions.description: This sanctuary in the Mae Taeng Valley lets you observe rescued elephants roaming freely through forested hillsides while learning about their individual stories and rehabilitation. The chance to prepare nutritious meal baskets and hand-feed these gentle giants at close range creates genuinely meaningful connections.
attractions.local_name: เอทิคัล เอเลเฟ่นท์ แซงชัวรี เชียงใหม่ บาย บิ๊กบอย
attractions.tip: Book the earliest morning slot to see the elephants at their most active during feeding time, and wear closed-toe shoes suitable for muddy terrain around the forest enclosures.

attractions.name: Elephant Pride Sanctuary
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in Mae Taeng Valley lets you observe rescued elephants roaming freely through forested terrain, bathing in natural streams, and interacting with their herds. The chance to witness these gentle giants displaying natural behaviors in a cage-free environment makes it genuinely rewarding.
attractions.local_name: เอเลเฟ่นท์ ไพรด์ แซงชัวรี
attractions.tip: Visit during morning feeding time when elephants are most active and playful. Wear shoes that can get muddy and bring a change of clothes if participating in mud spa activities.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Elephant Sanctuary
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in Mae Taeng Valley lets visitors feed, bathe, and walk alongside rescued elephants in their natural forest habitat without riding. The chance to observe these gentle giants' authentic behaviors and personalities up close creates genuine connections impossible in traditional tourist camps.
attractions.local_name: เชียงใหม่เอเลเฟ่นท์แซงชัวรี
attractions.tip: Book the morning session to see elephants at their most active during feeding time, and wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy during the river bathing experience.

attractions.name: Hidden Village Chiang Mai
attractions.description: This interactive cultural attraction recreates traditional Lanna village life with hands-on activities like bamboo weaving, traditional cooking, and rice farming in a lush garden setting. The authentic village setup with friendly local artisans offering genuine cultural exchange makes it perfect for families seeking meaningful interaction beyond typical tourist spots.
attractions.local_name: Hidden Village เชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Arrive early afternoon when craftspeople are most active and willing to teach. Wear clothes you don't mind getting dirty for rice planting activities.

attractions.name: Memories of Chiang Mai
attractions.description: This charming boutique shop in the Old City specializes in handcrafted local souvenirs, vintage-style postcards, and artisanal gifts that capture Chiang Mai's essence. The carefully curated collection of unique mementos and photo-worthy displays makes it perfect for finding distinctive keepsakes you won't see elsewhere.
attractions.local_name: ความทรงจำของเชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Visit during weekday mornings when the shop is quieter and staff have more time to share stories about the local artisans behind each product.

attractions.name: Wat Lok Moli
attractions.description: Buddhist temple dating to at least the 1300s & featuring a prominent chedi (stratified tower).

attractions.name: Changthong Heritage Park
attractions.description: This heritage park along the old city moat showcases traditional Lanna architecture and peaceful garden spaces beneath the shade of mature trees. Visit for serene waterside views that capture Chiang Mai's historic charm without the crowds of major temple sites.
attractions.local_name: อุทยานมรดกช้างท่อง
attractions.tip: Visit in early morning when local residents practice tai chi by the moat—the soft light creates perfect photo conditions and you'll experience the park as locals do.

attractions.name: จุดชมวิวดาดฟ้าเมญ่า
attractions.description: This rooftop viewpoint in the old city offers sweeping 360-degree views over Chiang Mai's ancient temples, moat, and surrounding mountains. The elevated perspective captures the historic city layout framed by Doi Suthep, particularly striking during golden hour.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวดาดฟ้าเมญ่า
attractions.tip: Arrive 30 minutes before sunset to secure a good spot and watch the sky change colors over the temple rooftops without the midday heat.

attractions.name: One Nimman
attractions.description: Retail shops, cafes & food stalls share space in a modern marketplace with big windows & a hip vibe.

attractions.name: Jungle Flight Chiang Mai
attractions.description: Soar through the rainforest canopy on ziplines stretching up to 1,000 meters, with aerial bridges and rappels connecting ancient trees. The longest zipline course in northern Thailand delivers pure adrenaline while spotting wildlife from perspectives few visitors experience.
attractions.local_name: จังเกิ้ล ไฟลท์ เชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Book the earliest morning slot to avoid midday heat and enjoy cooler temperatures with better chances of seeing birds and gibbons active in the canopy.

attractions.name: Maya Square
attractions.description: This modern lifestyle mall in the Nimman area combines sleek outdoor courtyards with chic cafes, boutiques, and a creative workspace vibe that attracts Chiang Mai's digital nomads and young professionals. The photogenic white architecture and tropical greenery create Instagram-worthy backdrops around every corner.
attractions.local_name: มายาสแควร์
attractions.tip: Visit weekday afternoons when the outdoor seating areas are less crowded and perfect for laptop work with free WiFi from surrounding cafes.

attractions.name: The wise big tree
attractions.description: This ancient banyan tree in the Nimman area has sprawling aerial roots and a canopy that creates a natural cathedral of shade, drawing locals who believe it holds spiritual wisdom. The perfect Instagram backdrop combines dramatic natural architecture with peaceful energy that makes you slow down and breathe.
attractions.local_name: ต้นไม้ใหญ่ผู้มีปัญญา
attractions.tip: Visit early morning around 7am when the light filters beautifully through the branches and before the heat intensifies—locals often leave small offerings at the base.

attractions.name: KLĀY cafe
attractions.description: This contemporary café in Nimman showcases minimalist Scandinavian-inspired design with clean concrete lines, abundant natural light, and carefully curated greenery perfect for photography. The artisan coffee paired with homemade pastries makes it a favorite among digital nomads and design enthusiasts.
attractions.local_name: KLĀY cafe
attractions.tip: Visit on weekday mornings between 9-11am to avoid crowds and get the best lighting for photos, especially near the corner window seats with plant backdrops.

attractions.name: Graph One Nimman
attractions.description: This modern mixed-use complex in Nimman features striking geometric architecture with white minimalist lines that create dramatic shadows and angles perfect for photography. The open-air design connects boutique shops, trendy cafes, and creative spaces across multiple levels with photogenic staircases and terraces.
attractions.local_name: กราฟ วัน นิมมาน
attractions.tip: Visit late afternoon when the sun creates the best shadows for photos. The rooftop areas and geometric staircases on the upper floors are less crowded than ground level.

attractions.name: Think Park
attractions.description: This open-air lifestyle space in Nimman combines trendy cafes, indie shops, and art installations across landscaped gardens and modern architecture. The relaxed outdoor setting with creative pop-up vendors and photogenic corners makes it perfect for a casual afternoon browse.
attractions.local_name: ธิงค์ พาร์ค
attractions.tip: Visit on weekends when local designers set up pop-up stalls selling handmade crafts, vintage clothing, and artisan goods you won't find in regular Nimman shops.

attractions.name: Huen Muan Jai
attractions.description: Well-known cafe serving traditional local fare in a restored wooden villa that has a garden.

attractions.name: Huge old tree
attractions.description: This centuries-old giant tree stands as a quiet sentinel in the Nimman area, its massive trunk and sprawling canopy creating a natural refuge amid the urban buzz. The peaceful atmosphere and unique photo angles between its enormous roots make it a refreshing escape from shopping and cafes.
attractions.local_name: ต้นไม้ใหญ่เก่าแก่
attractions.tip: Visit in early morning when soft light filters through the branches perfectly for photography, and the area is quiet before nearby cafes open.

attractions.name: Wat Jedyod Royal Temple Wat Chet Yod Phra Aram Luang
attractions.description: Those born in the year of the snake journey to this unique, historical Buddhist temple with artwork.

attractions.name: Jing Jai Market Chiang Mai
attractions.description: Lively weekend market supplying organic fruits & vegetables, plus food, beverages & handicrafts.

attractions.name: Ganesha Shrine Chiang Mai Arcade
attractions.description: This intimate Hindu shrine tucked within Chiang Mai Arcade features a golden Ganesha statue adorned with fresh marigold garlands and surrounded by the sweet scent of incense. Locals and devotees visit to pray for prosperity and remove obstacles, making it a genuine spiritual spot amid the commercial arcade.
attractions.local_name: ศาลพระพิฆเนศ เชียงใหม่ อาเขต
attractions.tip: Visit early morning when monks and local shopkeepers come to make offerings, creating an authentic atmosphere before the arcade gets busy with tourists.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai PAO Park
attractions.description: This hilltop public park features lush gardens, walking trails, and panoramic views over Chiang Mai's old city and surrounding mountains. The peaceful green space offers a refreshing escape from the heat with shaded pathways and well-maintained outdoor fitness equipment.
attractions.local_name: สวนเฉลิมพระเกียรติ จ.เชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Visit in the early morning around 6-7 AM to join local residents doing tai chi and exercise routines, when the air is coolest and clearest for photography.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai P.A.O. Public Park
attractions.description: This expansive Provincial Administrative Organization park features serene lakes dotted with water lilies, shaded jogging paths, and open lawns popular with local families practicing tai chi at dawn. Come for the peaceful atmosphere away from tourist crowds and the chance to observe authentic Chiang Mai daily life.
attractions.local_name: สวนสาธารณะ อบจ.เชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Visit during weekday mornings to see elderly groups doing traditional exercises and locals feeding fish at the lakes—a glimpse into community routines rarely seen by tourists.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Chaloem Phrakiat Park
attractions.description: This hillside park features winding trails through pine forest and native gardens, with elevated viewpoints overlooking Chiang Mai's eastern valleys and mountain ranges. The peaceful grounds offer a rare escape from the old city's tourist crowds, just minutes from the Sunday Walking Street area.
attractions.local_name: สวนเฉลิมพระเกียรติเชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Visit in early morning when local joggers and tai chi practitioners gather, and the air is cool and clear for the best mountain visibility before afternoon haze sets in.

attractions.name: Doi Pui Viewpoint
attractions.description: This mountain viewpoint perched at 1,685 meters offers sweeping panoramas across Chiang Mai's valley, temples, and distant mountain ranges framed by cool pine forests. The crisp air and layered vistas make it an ideal escape from the city heat below.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวดอยปุย
attractions.tip: Visit in the early morning during cool season (November-February) when mist clears to reveal the sharpest views and temperatures are most comfortable for the altitude.

attractions.name: Huay Tueng Thao Sculpture Park
attractions.description: This outdoor sculpture park showcases contemporary Thai art installations scattered across grassy grounds near Huay Tueng Thao reservoir, featuring oversized abstract pieces and whimsical figures perfect for creative photography. The relaxed open-air setting offers a refreshing cultural escape from Chiang Mai's temple circuits without the crowds.
attractions.local_name: อุทยานประติมากรรมห้วยเตี้ยงเฒ่า
attractions.tip: Visit in late afternoon when the light is softer for photos and the temperature cooler. The park is less maintained than typical tourist sites, so wear closed shoes.

attractions.name: The Canopy Walks
attractions.description: Long walkway suspended above the forest canopy, with views of mountains & the gardens below.

attractions.name: Montha Than Waterfall Trailhead
attractions.description: This trailhead marks the beginning of a moderate jungle hike through dense forest leading to a secluded multi-tiered waterfall on Doi Suthep's slopes. The trail offers a quieter alternative to crowded tourist waterfalls, with rewarding natural pools perfect for cooling off after the trek.
attractions.local_name: จุดเริ่มต้นเส้นทางน้ำตกมณฑาธาร
attractions.tip: Start early in the morning to avoid afternoon heat and bring proper hiking shoes as the trail can be slippery near stream crossings, especially during rainy season.

attractions.name: Doi Suthep
attractions.description: 5,499-foot mountain featuring trails through evergreen forests & a picturesque, hilltop temple.

attractions.name: Hmong Doi Pui Village
attractions.description: Hillside village set in a lush national park, home to a Hmong tribe, who sell traditional crafts.

attractions.name: Doi Hua Moo Viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint along the winding road to Doi Suthep offers sweeping panoramas of Chiang Mai city sprawling across the valley floor, framed by layered mountain ridges. The clear morning light reveals every detail of the urban landscape below, making it the perfect spot for capturing the scale of northern Thailand's largest city.
attractions.local_name: ดอยหัวหมู
attractions.tip: Arrive before 7am to catch the sunrise and beat tour buses. Park carefully on the roadside shoulder as there's limited designated parking space.

attractions.name: San Ku
attractions.description: This quiet agricultural village nestled on the slopes of Doi Suthep mountain offers glimpses of traditional northern Thai rural life amid rice paddies and flower farms. The peaceful countryside setting and photogenic mountain backdrop provide an authentic escape from Chiang Mai's tourist center.
attractions.local_name: สันกู่
attractions.tip: Visit during early morning hours when local farmers are working the fields and the light is perfect for photography with mountain views.

attractions.name: Doi Pui Nature Trail
attractions.description: This forested trail winds through montane jungle at 1,300 meters elevation, passing Hmong hill tribe villages and terraced vegetable gardens before reaching viewpoints over Chiang Mai valley. The cool mountain air and encounters with local farmers tending their crops offer an authentic glimpse of highland life just beyond the city.
attractions.local_name: เส้นทางศึกษาธรรมชาติดอยปุย
attractions.tip: Start early morning when mist clings to the forest canopy and temperatures are coolest. Wear proper hiking shoes as the trail becomes slippery during rainy season.

attractions.name: Doi Pui Waterfall Garden
attractions.description: A serene hillside garden wrapped around a multi-tiered waterfall on the slopes of Doi Suthep, where cool mountain streams cascade through manicured grounds and native forest. The refreshing mist and natural pools offer a peaceful escape from Chiang Mai's heat, perfect for a short nature break after visiting the nearby temple.
attractions.local_name: สวนน้ำตกดอยปุย
attractions.tip: Visit on weekday mornings to have the waterfall practically to yourself. Wear proper shoes as rocks around the falls can be slippery, and bring mosquito repellent for the shaded areas.

attractions.name: Doi Pui Summit
attractions.description: The highest accessible peak on Doi Suthep mountain reaches 1,685 meters, offering panoramic views over Chiang Mai's valleys and distant mountain ranges through cool, pine-scented air. The quiet summit, far less crowded than nearby Doi Suthep Temple, provides the clearest sunrise vistas in the area.
attractions.local_name: ดอยปุย
attractions.tip: Arrive before 7am to see the sea of mist blanketing the valleys below. Bring a jacket even in hot season as temperatures drop significantly at this elevation.

attractions.name: Mon Pha Dham Viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint perched on Doi Suthep mountain reveals sweeping panoramas of Chiang Mai's urban valleys framed by mountain ranges, with morning mist often pooling below like a white ocean. The uncrowded viewing platform offers photo opportunities that capture the dramatic scale of northern Thailand's landscape without the tourist crowds of nearby temples.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวมอญผาแดง
attractions.tip: Arrive around 6:30 AM during cool season for the best chance to photograph the sea of mist phenomenon. Bring a light jacket as temperatures drop significantly at this elevation.

attractions.name: Mae Sa Elephant Camp
attractions.description: Attraction offering elephant shows, elephant painting & experiences such as bathing & riding.

attractions.name: Royal Pavilion
attractions.description: Sprawling palace compound in lush, hillside surrounds known for its enshrined emerald Buddha.

attractions.name: Chom Cafe and Restaurant
attractions.description: Laid-back operation serving stir-fry, salads & sharing plates, plus desserts & coffee drinks.

attractions.name: Wat Phra That Doi Suthep
attractions.description: In addition to its gold stupa, pagodas & museum, this mountainside temple offers broad city views.

attractions.name: Doi Suthep-Pui National Park
attractions.description: Forested national park featuring a Buddhist temple, Bhubing Palace, wildlife, waterfalls & more.

attractions.name: Royal Park Rajapruek
attractions.description: Landscaped grounds featuring flowers, plants & sculptures, plus an elegant commemorative chapel.

attractions.name: Ob Khan National Park
attractions.description: A riverside gorge carved by turquoise waters flowing through massive granite boulders creates natural pools perfect for wading and picnicking under shaded forest canopy. The shallow rapids and easy walking trails make this a refreshing escape from Chiang Mai's heat without the crowds of more famous waterfalls.
attractions.local_name: อุทยานแห่งชาติโอบขาน
attractions.tip: Visit on weekdays to avoid local weekend crowds. Wear water shoes as the smooth rocks can be slippery, and bring snacks since food vendors are limited inside the park.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Night Safari
attractions.description: Large zoo with many animal species, day & evening tram safari rides, kids' activities & shows.

attractions.name: Kew Mae Pan nature trail
attractions.description: Guided nature trail through a forest featuring a waterfall & observation areas with scenic views.

attractions.name: Doi Inthanon National Park
attractions.description: Trails offers scenic views in this popular national park with pagodas near the summit & waterfalls.

attractions.name: Kew Mae Pan Viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated nature trail winds through moss-draped rhododendron forests and alpine meadows at 2,200 meters, revealing panoramic views of Doi Inthanon's peaks and valleys. The cool mountain air and unique high-altitude ecosystem offer a refreshing escape from Chiang Mai's tropical heat.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวแควแม่ปาน
attractions.tip: Start at 6:30 AM when park gates open to catch sunrise mist and avoid afternoon clouds that often obscure views. Hire a mandatory local Karen guide at the trailhead.

attractions.name: Morning Market @BaanKangWat
attractions.description: This creative community market at BaanKangWat showcases handmade crafts, artisanal foods, and unique designer goods from local Chiang Mai makers in a relaxed garden setting. The carefully curated selection of independent vendors offers one-of-a-kind products you won't find in typical tourist markets.
attractions.local_name: ตลาดเช้า @บ้านกางวัฒน์
attractions.tip: Arrive by 8am for the freshest baked goods and first pick of handmade items, as popular vendors often sell out by mid-morning.

attractions.name: Baan Kang Wat
attractions.description: This artisan village features charming ceramic studios, galleries, and cafes nestled in traditional wooden houses surrounding a peaceful courtyard. The handcrafted pottery and unique ceramic pieces made by local artists offer authentic souvenirs you won't find in tourist markets.
attractions.local_name: บ้านข้างวัด
attractions.tip: Visit on weekday mornings when artists are actively working in their studios and more willing to chat about their craft and techniques.

attractions.name: Wat Pha Lat
attractions.description: Small, statue-rich Buddhist temple & monastery hidden in a woodland & reached via a hiking trail.

attractions.name: Doi Suthep Hiking Trail (Wat Pha Lat)
attractions.description: This forested trail winds through moss-covered ruins and ancient Buddha statues hidden among ferns and jungle streams, leading to the serene Wat Pha Lat temple. The peaceful monastery atmosphere and dramatic temple structures built into the mountainside offer a mystical alternative to crowded Doi Suthep summit.
attractions.local_name: เส้นทางเดินป่าดอยสุเทพ (วัดผาลาด)
attractions.tip: Start early from Chiang Mai University's back gate to avoid afternoon heat. Wear proper hiking shoes as the trail gets slippery after rain, and bring insect repellent for the shaded forest sections.

attractions.name: จุดถ่ายภาพ พระธาตุดอยสุเทพและเมืองเชียงใหม่
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint on Doi Suthep mountain offers sweeping panoramas of the golden Wat Phra That temple spire framed against Chiang Mai's sprawling cityscape below. The prime spot for capturing the contrast between sacred mountain temple and modern urban valley in a single frame.
attractions.local_name: จุดถ่ายภาพ พระธาตุดอยสุเทพและเมืองเชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Arrive between 4-5pm when afternoon light bathes the golden chedi and city haze clears for sharper photos. Walk 50 meters past the main temple platform for less crowded angles.

attractions.name: Wat Phra That Doi Kham
attractions.description: Temple atop a forested mountain featuring ornate gold carvings & a 17-m. sitting Buddha statue.

attractions.name: Doikham Panorama View Point
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint on Doi Suthep Mountain offers sweeping vistas across Chiang Mai's valley and mountain ranges, with cool mountain air and pine tree surroundings. The panoramic views at sunset transform the entire valley into a canvas of golden light and shadow.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวดอยคำพาโนราม่า
attractions.tip: Arrive 45 minutes before sunset to secure the best viewing spot along the railing, and bring a light jacket as temperatures drop quickly once the sun goes down.

attractions.name: Viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated vantage point on Doi Suthep Mountain offers sweeping panoramas across Chiang Mai's valley, with the city grid visible below and mountain ranges stretching to the horizon. The prime opportunity here is capturing golden hour photographs when the valley glows in amber light and temple spires catch the last sun rays.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิว
attractions.tip: Arrive 45 minutes before sunset to secure the best spot along the railing, as local photographers and tour groups fill the space quickly during peak viewing times.

attractions.name: Elephant Jungle Sanctuary Chiang Mai (Grand Canyon Branch)
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary near the Grand Canyon water park lets you feed, bathe, and walk alongside rescued elephants in their natural forest habitat. The highlight is wading into mud pools with these gentle giants, creating unforgettable close encounters while supporting responsible elephant tourism.
attractions.local_name: เอเลเฟ่นท์ จังเกิล แซงชัวรี่ เชียงใหม่ (แกรนด์แคนยอน)
attractions.tip: Book the morning session to avoid afternoon heat and see elephants at their most active during feeding time. Wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy and bring a waterproof phone case.

attractions.name: Elephant Freedom Project, Elephant Sanctuary Chiang Mai
attractions.description: This ethical sanctuary in Mae Taeng Valley lets you observe rescued elephants roaming freely through forest and river habitats while learning about their rehabilitation stories. Walking alongside these gentle giants in their natural environment offers an intimate, observation-based experience without riding or performances.
attractions.local_name: โครงการเสรีภาพช้าง, เชียงใหม่
attractions.tip: Book the morning session when elephants are most active bathing in the river. Wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy and bring a waterproof phone case for photos.

attractions.name: Mon Jam
attractions.description: Mountaintop farming community with panoramic views, accommodations, shops & on-site meals.

attractions.name: Sri Lanna National Park
attractions.description: This urban national park preserves remnants of ancient Lanna forest within Chiang Mai's city limits, featuring walking trails through teak and dipterocarp trees alongside Huay Kaew stream. The park offers an easy escape into nature without leaving town, perfect for a morning walk where monkeys and squirrels roam freely among Buddhist shrines.
attractions.local_name: อุทยานแห่งชาติศรีลานนา
attractions.tip: Start at the Wat Pha Lat trailhead near Chiang Mai University for the most accessible route. Arrive early morning to avoid heat and catch monks collecting alms along the forest paths.

attractions.name: Dantewada Land of Angels Waterfall Park
attractions.description: A landscaped waterfall park on Doi Suthep mountain featuring tiered cascades, stone pathways, and tropical gardens designed for photo opportunities. The multi-level viewing platforms offer close-up access to the falls without challenging hikes, perfect for capturing that Instagram-worthy shot with minimal effort.
attractions.local_name: ดันเตวาดา แลนด์ ออฟ แองเจิลส์ วอเตอร์ฟอลล์ พาร์ค
attractions.tip: Visit on weekday mornings to avoid tour groups and get clear shots of the waterfalls. The lower tier has the best light between 9-11am for photography.

attractions.name: Elephant Nature Park
attractions.description: Elephant rescue & rehabilitation center with volunteer opportunities, jungle tours & rafting.

attractions.name: Elephant Inwild Sanctuary
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in the Mae Taeng Valley allows visitors to observe rescued elephants roaming freely through forest and river habitats without riding or performances. Walking alongside these gentle giants as they forage and bathe in their natural environment creates meaningful wildlife encounters.
attractions.local_name: เอเลเฟ่นท์ อินไวล์ แซงชัวรี
attractions.tip: Book the morning session to see elephants most active during feeding time, and wear quick-dry clothing as you'll likely get splashed during river bathing activities.

attractions.name: Elephant Rescue Park
attractions.description: This ethical sanctuary in the Mae Taeng Valley allows rescued elephants to roam freely while visitors observe their natural behaviors from respectful distances. Walk alongside these gentle giants as they forage, bathe in mud pits, and interact with their herd in a truly cage-free environment.
attractions.local_name: อุทยานช่วยเหลือช้าง
attractions.tip: Book the morning session when elephants are most active and playful. Wear closed-toe shoes you don't mind getting muddy, as you'll walk through forest trails alongside the herd.

attractions.name: Maerim Elephant Sanctuary
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in the Mae Taeng Valley lets visitors observe and feed rescued elephants in their natural forest habitat without riding. The chance to walk alongside these gentle giants as they bathe in the river creates an intimate, memorable encounter.
attractions.local_name: แม่ริมเอเลเฟ่นท์แซงชัวรี
attractions.tip: Book the morning slot to see elephants at their most active during feeding time, and wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy during the river bathing session.

attractions.name: CHIANGMAI ELEPHANT CARE (Mae Taeng)
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in Mae Taeng Valley lets you feed, bathe, and walk alongside rescued elephants in their natural forest habitat without riding or exploitation. The intimate group sizes ensure meaningful interactions with these gentle giants while supporting their conservation and rehabilitation.
attractions.local_name: เชียงใหม่ เอเลเฟ่นท์ แคร์
attractions.tip: Wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy and bring a change of clothes—bathing elephants in the river gets everyone thoroughly soaked and covered in clay.

attractions.name: Doi Lom Elephant Rescue Sanctuary
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in the Mae Taeng Valley allows rescued elephants to roam freely through forested hillsides while visitors observe their natural behavior from a respectful distance. The experience focuses on learning about elephant conservation and watching these gentle giants interact, bathe, and feed in their natural habitat without riding or shows.
attractions.local_name: ดอยลมช้างกู้ภัย
attractions.tip: Book the morning session to see elephants most active during feeding time, and wear closed-toe shoes as paths through the forest can be muddy and uneven.

attractions.name: Chiang Mai Mountain Sanctuary tours (Elephant)
attractions.description: This Mae Taeng Valley sanctuary allows visitors to observe rescued elephants roaming freely in forested surroundings, with opportunities to feed and bathe them in natural streams. The ethical approach focuses on observation over riding, making it ideal for families seeking meaningful wildlife encounters.
attractions.local_name: เชียงใหม่เมาน์เทนแซงชัวรี
attractions.tip: Book the morning session to see elephants at their most active during feeding time, and wear quick-dry clothes as the stream bathing portion gets everyone thoroughly soaked.

attractions.name: Baan Chang Elephant Park
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in the Mae Taeng Valley focuses on observation-based interactions where visitors watch rescued elephants roam, bathe, and socialize in a natural forest setting. The park's commitment to no-riding policies and smaller group sizes creates meaningful encounters with these gentle giants.
attractions.local_name: บ้านช้างเอเลเฟ่นท์พาร์ค
attractions.tip: Book the morning session to see elephants at their most active during feeding and bath time, when they're playful and engaging with each other.

attractions.name: Maetaeng Elephant Park & Clinic
attractions.description: This working elephant clinic in the Mae Taeng Valley combines conservation efforts with visitor experiences, allowing close encounters with rescued elephants in a rural hillside setting. The opportunity to observe veterinary care and rehabilitation of elephants makes this a meaningful alternative to traditional elephant camps.
attractions.local_name: สวนช้างและคลินิกแม่แตง
attractions.tip: Visit during morning feeding times when elephants are most active and the veterinary team conducts health checks, offering genuine insights into elephant care and medical treatment.

attractions.name: Elephant EcoValley
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in Mae Taeng Valley lets you observe rescued elephants bathing, foraging, and interacting in their natural habitat without riding or performances. The chance to prepare natural food supplements and watch elephants roam freely across forested hills offers an authentic conservation experience.
attractions.local_name: เอเลเฟ่นท์ อีโค วัลเลย์
attractions.tip: Book the morning session to see elephants most active during feeding time, and wear dark-colored clothes as the mud from the bathing area stains easily.

attractions.name: Elephant Retirement Park (Camp)
attractions.description: This ethical sanctuary in the Mae Taeng Valley allows rescued elephants to roam freely while visitors observe their natural behaviors, feed them, and walk alongside these gentle giants without riding. The highlight is bathing with elephants in a natural river setting, creating an intimate connection that respects their dignity.
attractions.local_name: ค่ายเกษียณช้าง
attractions.tip: Visit in the morning when elephants are most active and playful. Wear dark-colored clothes as mud stains are inevitable, and bring a change of clothes for after the river bath.

attractions.name: Thai Elephant Home
attractions.description: Attraction for meeting, riding & swimming with rescued elephants, plus a breeding program & treks.

attractions.name: Elephant nature lovers sanctuary
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in Mae Taeng Valley lets you observe rescued elephants roaming freely in their natural forest habitat, bathing in streams and foraging without riding or performances. The intimate, camera-ready encounters with gentle giants in lush surroundings create unforgettable moments while supporting genuine elephant welfare.
attractions.local_name: เอเลเฟ่นท์ เนเจอร์ เลิฟเวอร์ส แซงชัวรี
attractions.tip: Wear dark-colored, quick-dry clothes as you'll get muddy during elephant feeding and bathing activities. Bring a waterproof phone case for close-up photos at the stream.

attractions.name: Ran-Tong Save & Rescue Elephant Centre
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in Mae Taeng Valley focuses on rescue and rehabilitation, allowing visitors to observe elephants bathing in natural streams and roaming freely through forested terrain. The chance to feed and walk alongside rescued elephants in their natural habitat, without riding or performances, offers an authentic connection with these gentle giants.
attractions.local_name: ศูนย์ช่วยเหลือและอนุรักษ์ช้างรานทอง
attractions.tip: Visit during morning feeding time around 9 AM when elephants are most active and playful. Wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy, as the bathing experience can get messy.

attractions.name: New Elephant Home
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in the Mae Taeng Valley focuses on rescued elephants roaming freely through forested grounds alongside a natural river. Interact with these gentle giants through feeding and bathing activities that prioritize animal welfare over entertainment.
attractions.local_name: บ้านช้างใหม่
attractions.tip: Book the morning session to see elephants at their most active during feeding time, and wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy during the river bathing experience.

attractions.name: Happy Elephant Home
attractions.description: This ethical sanctuary in Mae Taeng Valley lets you observe rescued elephants roaming freely through natural forest habitat, bathing in streams, and foraging without forced performances. Walking alongside these gentle giants as they interact naturally with their mahouts and environment offers an intimate glimpse into elephant behavior rarely seen at traditional camps.
attractions.local_name: แฮปปี้ เอเลเฟ่นท์ โฮม
attractions.tip: Visit during morning feeding time when elephants are most active and playful. Wear dark-colored clothes as the red soil along walking paths stains easily.

attractions.name: Lanna Kingdom Elephant Sanctuary
attractions.description: This ethical sanctuary in Mae Taeng Valley lets you observe rescued elephants roaming freely through forest terrain, bathing in natural streams, and foraging without riding or performance pressure. The chance to witness these gentle giants displaying natural behaviors in a conservation-focused environment that prioritizes their welfare over entertainment.
attractions.local_name: เลานนาคิงดอมเอเลเฟ่นท์แซงชัวรี
attractions.tip: Book the morning session when elephants are most active and playful, especially during feeding time. Wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy during the mud spa activity.

food.name: Garden to Table Chiangmai
food.where: 136 4 Ratchapakhinai Rd, Tambon Si Phum, Amphoe Mueang Chiang Mai, Chang Wat Chiang Mai 50200, Thailand
food.tip: Order their fresh herb salads and house-made pastries. Best visited early morning when ingredients are freshest.
food.local_name: สวนสู่โต๊ะ เชียงใหม่

food.name: Another World
food.where: T. Phra Singh, 22 Moon Muang, Tambon Phra Sing, Amphoe Mueang Chiang Mai, Chang Wat Chiang Mai 50200, Thailand
food.tip: Visit after sunset for the best atmosphere. Try their signature cocktails made with local spirits and seasonal fruits.
food.local_name: Another World

food.name: The Moat House
food.where: 3, 33 Mun Mueang Rd, Tambon Si Phum, Amphoe Mueang Chiang Mai, Chang Wat Chiang Mai 50200, Thailand
food.tip: Visit after sunset for the best atmosphere. Try their signature cocktails featuring local spirits and fresh tropical ingredients.
food.local_name: เดอะ โมท เฮาส์

food.name: Ikigai Restaurant
food.where: 203/6, ตําบล, Tambon Fa Ham, Amphoe Mueang Chiang Mai, Chang Wat Chiang Mai 50000, Thailand
food.tip: Order the omakase experience during dinner hours for the freshest preparations. Less crowded on weekday afternoons.
food.local_name: อิกิไก Restaurant

food.name: Ministry of Roasters - Chiang Mai
food.where: 199 Amphoe, Tambon Chang Moi, Amphoe Mueang Chiang Mai, Chang Wat Chiang Mai 50300, Thailand
food.tip: Order their signature single-origin pour-over coffee. Best visited early morning before 9am for quieter atmosphere and freshest roasts.
food.local_name: มินิสตรี่ ออฟ โรสเตอร์ส

food.name: Caramellow Cafe
food.where: Caramellow, 223 Thanon Mahidol, Tambon Pa Daet, Amphoe Mueang Chiang Mai, Chang Wat Chiang Mai 50100, Thailand
food.tip: Try their signature caramel-infused coffee drinks in the morning. Best visited early to enjoy the peaceful old-city atmosphere.
food.local_name: คาราเมลโลว์ คาเฟ่

food.name: Tha Phae Walking Street
food.where: Rachadamnoen Rd, Tambon Si Phum, Amphoe Mueang Chiang Mai, Chang Wat Chiang Mai 50200, Thailand
food.tip: Each Sunday afternoon until midnight this street becomes a gallery for local arts & crafts.

food.name: Wua Lai Walking Street Saturday Market

food.tip: Outdoor bazaar with vendors selling traditional street snacks, handmade crafts & souvenirs.
