krabi.name: Krabi
krabi.tagline: Limestone Cliffs, Emerald Water, Pure Magic

attractions.name: Princess Cave
attractions.description: A sacred cave shrine nestled beneath towering limestone cliffs where local fishermen leave wooden phallus offerings to the fertility goddess for safe passage at sea. The dramatic setting between jungle and turquoise waters, combined with its unique cultural tradition, creates an unexpectedly memorable stop along Railay's coastal trails.
attractions.local_name: ถ้ำพระนาง
attractions.tip: Visit at low tide when you can walk along the beach from Railay West in about 15 minutes instead of taking a longtail boat.

attractions.name: Ao Nang beach
attractions.description: This long crescent beach sits beneath dramatic limestone karsts that rise vertically from the sand, with longtail boats bobbing in turquoise waters. The perfect base for island-hopping day trips to the famous Four Islands and beyond.
attractions.local_name: หาดอ่าวนาง
attractions.tip: Visit the northern end of the beach near the Walking Street for calmer waters and fewer crowds, especially during high season when the main stretch gets busy.

attractions.name: Phra Nang Beach
attractions.description: This sandy beach backdropped by limestone cliffs with caves features clear water with small islands.

attractions.name: Monkey Trail, Ao Nang
attractions.description: Path through the jungle with wooden steps & rickety bridges, popular for monkey spotting & sunsets.

attractions.name: East Railay Viewpoint
attractions.description: This steep jungle trail climbs through rocky terrain and twisted roots to a lookout perched above the limestone cliffs, revealing the turquoise waters separating East and West Railay below. The challenging 30-minute ascent rewards hikers with sweeping views over Krabi's karst formations and the Andaman Sea.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวอีสต์ไร่เลย์
attractions.tip: Start the climb early morning before 8am to avoid heat and crowds. Wear proper hiking shoes with grip as the trail involves rope-assisted sections over slippery rocks.

attractions.name: Hidden beach
attractions.description: This secluded cove tucked between limestone cliffs requires a short kayak or longtail boat ride from Ao Nang, keeping crowds minimal even during peak season. The shallow turquoise waters and soft sand create perfect conditions for peaceful swimming away from the busier beaches.
attractions.local_name: หาดซ่อนเร้น
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide when the beach expands significantly and small tidal pools form near the rocks, ideal for spotting small fish and crabs.

attractions.name: Bat Cave
attractions.description: A dramatic limestone cavern where thousands of bats emerge at dusk in swirling columns against the Krabi sunset. The cave itself features illuminated stalactites and a raised wooden walkway that leads through the mysterious chambers.
attractions.local_name: ถ้ำค้างคาว
attractions.tip: Visit between 5:30-6:00 PM to witness the bat exodus. Bring a flashlight for exploring darker sections and wear closed shoes as the cave floor can be slippery.

attractions.name: Ao Nang LandMark Beach
attractions.description: A quieter stretch of Ao Nang's coastline featuring limestone karst formations rising dramatically from the turquoise Andaman Sea, with soft sand ideal for lounging. The Instagram-worthy backdrop of towering cliffs framing the beach creates picture-perfect compositions without the crowds of the main beach area.
attractions.local_name: หาดอ่าวนาง แลนด์มาร์ค
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide in the late afternoon when the receding water reveals tidal pools and the western light illuminates the limestone cliffs perfectly for photography.

attractions.name: Tushi's Swing
attractions.description: A towering wooden swing perched on the hillside between Ao Nang and Nopparat Thara beaches offers sweeping views over the Andaman coastline and limestone karsts. The perfect spot for that signature Krabi photo with turquoise waters and dramatic cliffs as your backdrop.
attractions.local_name: ทูชิส์สวิง
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon for the best lighting and fewer crowds. Wear secure footwear for the short uphill walk and bring a sarong or light cover-up if wearing beachwear.

attractions.name: Lagoon
attractions.description: This serene lagoon near Ao Nang features emerald-green waters surrounded by dramatic limestone cliffs and mangrove forests, accessible by a short trail or kayak. The calm, shallow waters and natural rock formations create perfect conditions for photography and peaceful swimming away from the crowded beaches.
attractions.local_name: ทะเลสาบ
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide when the water is clearest and the lagoon floor reveals interesting rock patterns. Bring water shoes as the bottom can be rocky in places.

attractions.name: Ko Rang Beach
attractions.description: This secluded beach near Ao Nang features powdery white sand framed by dramatic limestone cliffs and crystal-clear turquoise waters ideal for snorkeling. The vibrant coral reefs just meters from shore teem with colorful tropical fish, making it perfect for underwater photography.
attractions.local_name: หาดเกาะรัง
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide when the exposed reef pools create natural aquariums where you can observe small fish and sea creatures without needing to swim.

attractions.name: Lomtalay Aonang
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint overlooking Ao Nang's coastline offers panoramic vistas of limestone karsts rising from turquoise waters, with West Railay Beach visible in the distance. The relatively easy climb rewards visitors with one of Krabi's most photogenic sunset backdrops, framed by tropical vegetation.
attractions.local_name: ลมทะเลอ่าวนาง
attractions.tip: Arrive 45 minutes before sunset to secure the best photo spot and bring mosquito repellent. The trail can be slippery after rain, so wear proper footwear.

attractions.name: ภูเขาพระมหาโพธิสัตว์กวนอิน
attractions.description: A striking white Guanyin Bodhisattva statue stands atop a hillside overlooking Ao Nang, surrounded by smaller Buddhist shrines and prayer halls decorated in gold and red. The elevated viewpoint offers sweeping panoramas of Krabi's limestone karsts and the Andaman Sea, making the climb worthwhile.
attractions.local_name: ภูเขาพระมหาโพธิสัตว์กวนอิน
attractions.tip: Visit late afternoon when the heat subsides and the golden hour light enhances photos. Wear modest clothing covering shoulders and knees as this is an active worship site.

attractions.name: Ao Nang Beach Shrine
attractions.description: This colorful beachfront spirit house sits nestled among limestone rocks where locals leave daily offerings of flowers, incense, and fruit to honor protective sea spirits. The shrine offers a peaceful cultural moment just steps from the bustling beach, blending traditional Thai animist beliefs with dramatic natural surroundings.
attractions.local_name: ศาลเจ้าหาดอ่าวนาง
attractions.tip: Visit in early morning when local fishermen come to make offerings before heading out to sea—you'll witness authentic rituals and have the shrine mostly to yourself for photos.

attractions.name: Lore's Ancient tree within stalactites
attractions.description: A centuries-old tree grows impossibly through a cathedral of limestone stalactites, creating an otherworldly fusion of living wood and ancient stone formations. The surreal interplay between organic roots and crystallized minerals offers photographers and nature lovers a rare geological phenomenon found nowhere else in Krabi.
attractions.local_name: ต้นไม้โบราณในหินงอก
attractions.tip: Visit early morning when soft light filters through the cave opening, illuminating the tree without harsh shadows—ideal for photography and fewer tour groups.

attractions.name: Shell Cemetery
attractions.description: Shell cemetery with abundant shellfish fossils that have formed shapes like concrete blocks.

attractions.name: Cave Pra Nang Beach
attractions.description: A dramatic limestone cave frames this secluded beach where emerald waters lap against powder-soft sand, accessible only by longtail boat from Ao Nang or Railay. The cave itself creates a natural cathedral with sunlight streaming through openings, perfect for photography during late morning hours.
attractions.local_name: ถ้ำหาดพระนาง
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide between November and April for easiest cave access and calmer waters. Bring water shoes as sharp rocks line the cave entrance.

attractions.name: Railay West Beach
attractions.description: Expansive white-sand beach in lush jungle surrounds featuring soaring limestone cliffs & caves.

attractions.name: Railay Walking Street
attractions.description: This compact pedestrian path threads through Railay's limestone canyon, connecting the east and west beaches with a cluster of cafes, massage shops, and climbing gear outfitters tucked between towering karst walls. The charm lies in its relaxed beach-town atmosphere where barefoot travelers browse handmade jewelry and book tomorrow's island-hopping tours.
attractions.local_name: ถนนคนเดินอ่าวไร่เลย์
attractions.tip: Visit after sunset when fairy lights illuminate the path and restaurants set up tables outdoors. Prices for tours and activities are often better here than at beachfront resorts.

attractions.name: Railay stalactite
attractions.description: A dramatic natural cave formation where limestone stalactites hang from the ceiling like frozen waterfalls, accessible via a short but steep climb near Railay Beach. The otherworldly rock formations and filtered light create an atmospheric grotto perfect for photography and geological wonder.
attractions.local_name: หินงอกหินย้อยไร่เลย์
attractions.tip: Visit early morning before 9am to avoid crowds and heat. Wear proper shoes with grip as the path involves rope climbing on slippery limestone surfaces.

attractions.name: Thale Waek
attractions.description: A natural sandbar emerges at low tide, connecting three islands and allowing visitors to walk across the turquoise sea between Koh Dam Khwan, Koh Tup, and Koh Mor. The phenomenon creates a 250-meter pristine white sand pathway surrounded by shallow coral-rich waters perfect for snorkeling.
attractions.local_name: ทะเลแหวก
attractions.tip: Check tide tables and arrive 2 hours before low tide to watch the sandbar gradually appear and secure the best photo spots before tour groups arrive from Ao Nang.

attractions.name: Emerald Lagoon Railay hike
attractions.description: A steep, rope-assisted jungle trail leads to a hidden freshwater lagoon surrounded by towering limestone cliffs, where emerald-green water sits perfectly still like a secret pool. The challenging climb rewards you with one of Railay's most photographed natural wonders, accessible only to those willing to navigate muddy roots and near-vertical sections.
attractions.local_name: ทะเลสาบมรกตไร่เลย์
attractions.tip: Start early before 8am to avoid crowds and intense heat. Wear shoes with excellent grip as the trail gets muddy and slippery, especially near the rope sections.

attractions.name: Railay Beach cave
attractions.description: A dramatic limestone cave tucked into Railay's towering cliffs, featuring a wooden staircase leading to a small chamber adorned with colorful phallic offerings left by fishermen seeking fertility blessings. The cave's unique spiritual significance and the challenging climb reward visitors with an intimate glimpse into local beliefs and impressive rock formations.
attractions.local_name: ถ้ำไร่เลย์
attractions.tip: Visit early morning before 9am to avoid crowds and heat. Wear proper shoes as the wooden stairs can be slippery, and bring a headlamp to explore the darker recesses.

attractions.name: Diamond Cave / Phra Nang Nai Cave
attractions.description: A limestone cavern accessed via steep stairs, featuring impressive stalactite and stalagmite formations illuminated by colored lights that create dramatic shadows throughout the chambers. The elevated vantage point offers sweeping views across Railay's jungle canopy and karst cliffs, making the climb worthwhile.
attractions.local_name: ถ้ำเพชร / ถ้ำพระนางใน
attractions.tip: Wear proper shoes with grip as the wooden stairs can be slippery after rain. Visit early morning to avoid crowds and the midday heat that makes the steep climb more difficult.

attractions.name: Railay Viewpoint Resort
attractions.description: Colorful rooms & pagoda-style bungalows in a relaxed retreat with a pool, a dive center & dining.

attractions.name: Tub Islands
attractions.description: A tiny rocky outcrop connected to neighboring islands by stunning white sandbars that emerge during low tide, creating natural walkways across turquoise shallows. The sandbars offer some of Krabi's most photogenic scenes, where you can walk between three islands with water barely ankle-deep.
attractions.local_name: เกาะทับ
attractions.tip: Visit during December to April low tide mornings when sandbars are widest and most dramatic. Bring water shoes as coral fragments can be sharp on barefoot walking.

attractions.name: Wat Tham Suea
attractions.description: Expansive temple complex in lush jungle surrounds known for its gold Buddha statue & cave network.

attractions.name: Emerald Pool
attractions.description: Mineral pool & popular swimming spot in a lush forest with a wooden walking track.

attractions.name: Viewpoint at Tiger Cave Temple
attractions.description: Climb 1,237 steep steps through forest canopy to reach a golden Buddha statue crowning a limestone peak with 360-degree views over Krabi's karst landscape and Andaman coast. The challenging ascent rewards climbers with monkeys playing among ancient caves and one of southern Thailand's most expansive panoramas.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิววัดถ้ำเสือ
attractions.tip: Start your climb before 7am to avoid midday heat and crowds. Bring water and wear proper shoes as steps are uneven and slippery when wet.

attractions.name: Wat Mahathat Wachiramongkol (Wat Bang Thong)
attractions.description: This striking temple features a golden pagoda housing a Buddha relic, surrounded by intricate white and gold architecture that gleams under the Krabi sun. The elevated location offers sweeping views across Ao Nang's limestone karsts and coastline, making it a photographer's paradise.
attractions.local_name: วัดมหาธาตุวชิรมงคล (วัดบางทอง)
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon when tour groups have left and the golden light enhances the temple's metallic surfaces. Remove shoes before entering any building and dress modestly with covered shoulders and knees.

attractions.name: Stairs Wat Tham Suea
attractions.description: Challenge yourself on the grueling 1,237-step climb to Tiger Cave Temple's summit, passing through jungle canopy and past golden Buddha statues carved into limestone cliffs. The panoramic 360-degree views over Krabi's karst mountains and Andaman Sea from the peak reward those who complete the ascent.
attractions.local_name: บันไดวัดถ้ำเสือ
attractions.tip: Start your climb before 8am to avoid the midday heat and crowds. Bring a bottle with electrolytes and watch for cheeky monkeys near the base who snatch food and loose items.

attractions.name: Khao Khanab Nam
attractions.description: Twin limestone karsts rise dramatically from the Krabi River's edge, forming a natural gateway that's become the symbol of Krabi Town. Best experienced by longtail boat, which weaves between the cliffs and ventures into hidden caves adorned with ancient rock formations and stalactites.
attractions.local_name: เขาขนาบน้ำ
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide to access the caves inside the karsts. Local longtail boats from Chao Fah Pier offer better rates than tour operators at your hotel.

attractions.name: Khao Tham Suea Pahksa Sawan Krabi Urban Forest
attractions.description: This urban forest park features elevated wooden walkways weaving through limestone karst formations and native jungle vegetation, offering close-up views of Krabi's geological wonders. The peaceful trails and cave temples provide an accessible nature escape right within Krabi town limits.
attractions.local_name: เขาถ้ำเสือภักษะสวรรค์ ป่าเมืองกระบี่
attractions.tip: Visit early morning when monkeys are most active near the cave entrance, and bring water as there are limited facilities along the elevated boardwalk sections.

attractions.name: Tiger Cave temple
attractions.description: This Buddhist temple complex challenges visitors with 1,237 steep steps leading to a golden summit shrine surrounded by macaques and limestone cliffs. The reward at the top is a sweeping 360-degree view across Krabi's karst landscape and jungle canopy.
attractions.local_name: วัดถ้ำเสือ
attractions.tip: Start your climb before 8am to avoid the midday heat and crowds. Bring water and watch your belongings carefully around the monkeys who snatch unsecured items.

attractions.name: Krabi Elephant Shelter
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary near Ao Nang allows visitors to observe, feed, and bathe rescued elephants in a natural environment without riding or performing shows. Experience genuine interaction with these gentle giants while supporting their rehabilitation and conservation in the lush Krabi jungle.
attractions.local_name: ศูนย์พักพิงช้างกระบี่
attractions.tip: Visit during morning feeding time when elephants are most active and playful. Wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy and bring a waterproof phone case for the bathing session.

attractions.name: Dragon's Crest (Ngon Nak Nature Trail)
attractions.description: Steep climb through thick forest to a panoramic viewpoint overlooking peaks, beaches & islands.

attractions.name: The Hidden Gem Krabi
attractions.description: This lesser-known viewpoint and nature spot showcases Krabi's dramatic limestone karsts rising from emerald waters, with far fewer crowds than popular lookouts. The intimate setting offers perfect photo opportunities where you can capture the raw beauty of the Andaman coast without competing for space.
attractions.local_name: เดอะ ฮิดเดน เจม กระบี่
attractions.tip: Visit during golden hour around 5:30 PM when the light hits the karsts perfectly and the day-trippers have left for an almost private experience.

attractions.name: Noppharat Thara Beach
attractions.description: This expansive golden sand beach stretches for five kilometers with shallow, crystal-clear waters perfect for wading and dotted with traditional longtail boats. The connecting wooden pier offers unobstructed sunset views toward the Phi Phi Islands that turn the sky into brilliant shades of orange and pink.
attractions.local_name: หาดนพรัตน์ธารา
attractions.tip: Walk north along the beach at low tide to reach the rocky headland where locals collect shellfish—you'll have this entire section to yourself most afternoons.

attractions.name: View Point Hong Island
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint overlooks the emerald lagoon hidden within Hong Island's limestone cliffs, where turquoise waters create a natural amphitheater surrounded by vertical rock walls. The panoramic vista captures the entire lagoon system and scattered islands dotting Phang Nga Bay, making it Krabi's most photographed vantage point.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวเกาะห้อง
attractions.tip: Arrive with the first boat group around 9am before crowds surge. The steep climb takes 10 minutes with rope assists on slippery sections, so wear proper shoes with grip.

attractions.name: Rest Stop View point Khao Ngon Nak
attractions.description: This rest stop platform sits halfway up the challenging Khao Ngon Nak trail, offering dramatic limestone cliff views across the jungle canopy to the Andaman Sea. It's the perfect turnaround point for hikers who want rewarding panoramas without completing the full grueling ascent to the summit.
attractions.local_name: จุดพักชมวิวเขางอนนาก
attractions.tip: Start before 7am to reach this viewpoint while still cool and with softer light for photos. Bring at least 2 liters of water per person as there are no facilities along the trail.

attractions.name: Dragon Crest Mountain
attractions.description: A steep trail leads to this rock-ledge viewpoint overlooking a valley with greenery-clad hills.

attractions.name: Hat Noppharat Thara-Mu Ko Phi Phi National Park
attractions.description: National park including islands known for rugged cliffs, gorges, coral reefs & The Beach film.

attractions.name: Trail up to Kuan Nom Saow viewpoint
attractions.description: This steep jungle trail climbs through limestone forest to a dramatic clifftop platform overlooking Ao Nang's turquoise coastline and karst islands. The panoramic reward at the summit captures one of Krabi's most photogenic vantage points, perfect for sunrise or late afternoon light.
attractions.local_name: เส้นทางขึ้นจุดชมวิวควนหนมเสาว์
attractions.tip: Start early morning to avoid heat and crowds, and wear proper hiking shoes as the trail has steep sections with ropes. Bring water and allow 45 minutes up.

attractions.name: Klang Cave
attractions.description: This lesser-known cave near Railay requires a steep climb through jungle terrain and rock scrambling to reach its hidden entrance, rewarding adventurers with dramatic stalactites and a tranquil interior chamber. The challenging ascent filters out most tourists, leaving you with an authentic exploration experience away from Railay's busy beaches.
attractions.local_name: ถ้ำกลาง
attractions.tip: Wear proper hiking shoes with good grip as the trail becomes slippery after rain. Start early morning to avoid midday heat during the climb up.

attractions.name: Nong Thale Peak
attractions.description: This jungle-covered limestone peak rises dramatically between Ao Nang and Railay, offering a steep 45-minute climb through dense forest to a sweeping viewpoint over karst cliffs and turquoise waters. The challenging trail rewards hikers with one of Krabi's most dramatic panoramas, perfect for sunrise photography without the crowds.
attractions.local_name: ยอดเขาหนองทะเล
attractions.tip: Start before 7am to avoid heat and crowds. Bring gloves for the rope sections near the summit and watch for monkeys on the lower trail who may grab loose items.

attractions.name: The Elephant Sanctuary Krabi Thailand
attractions.description: This ethical sanctuary lets you walk alongside rescued elephants in their natural rainforest habitat, watching them bathe in mud pits and forage freely without riding or performances. The intimate small-group experience provides genuine insight into elephant behavior and conservation efforts while supporting their welfare.
attractions.local_name: เดอะ เอเลเฟ่นท์ แซงชัวรี กระบี่
attractions.tip: Visit during morning feeding time when elephants are most active and social. Wear closed shoes and bring a change of clothes as you'll walk through muddy terrain alongside the elephants.

attractions.name: The tree house cafe
attractions.description: This whimsical café features elevated wooden platforms built around towering trees, with cozy seating nooks suspended among the branches overlooking Krabi's limestone karsts. Come for the creative photo opportunities among the treehouse-style architecture that makes you feel like you're dining in a fairytale forest.
attractions.local_name: เดอะทรีเฮาส์คาเฟ่
attractions.tip: Visit during golden hour between 4-5pm when the soft light filters through the leaves perfectly, and the café is less crowded than lunch hours.

attractions.name: The Hideaway แอบซ่อนคาเฟ่
attractions.description: This charming garden café features lush tropical plants, vintage décor, and cozy seating nooks perfect for capturing that Instagram-worthy shot. The laid-back atmosphere and photogenic corners make it an ideal escape from Krabi Town's busy streets.
attractions.local_name: The Hideaway แอบซ่อนคาเฟ่
attractions.tip: Arrive before 10am on weekdays to have the best photo spots to yourself before the lunch crowd arrives.

attractions.name: Khao Ngon Nak Nature Trail
attractions.description: This challenging 3.5-kilometer trail climbs through dense jungle to a dramatic clifftop viewpoint overlooking Krabi's turquoise bay, limestone karsts, and scattered islands. The dragon-crest ridge at the summit offers one of southern Thailand's most rewarding panoramas after a steep 2-3 hour ascent.
attractions.local_name: เขางอนนาก
attractions.tip: Start by 7 AM to avoid midday heat and afternoon clouds that obscure views. Bring at least 2 liters of water per person and wear gloves for the rope sections.

attractions.name: Rai Island
attractions.description: This tiny limestone island ringed by powdery white sand sits between Poda and Tup islands, offering crystal-clear shallow waters ideal for snorkeling among colorful reef fish. The sandbar that emerges at low tide creates a natural bridge to neighboring islands, perfect for those memorable island-hopping photos.
attractions.local_name: เกาะไร่
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide (usually late morning) to walk across the exposed sandbar connecting to Tup Island, and bring water shoes as coral fragments can be sharp underfoot.

attractions.name: Park Ranger Unit 4 (Tub Kak)
attractions.description: This ranger station serves as a protected gateway to pristine island waters where limestone karsts rise dramatically from turquoise seas. The station provides the best vantage point for observing untouched marine ecosystems and serves as the jumping-off spot for exploring secluded islands in the Four Islands cluster.
attractions.local_name: หน่วยพิทักษ์อุทยานแห่งชาติที่ 4 (ทับกาก)
attractions.tip: Arrive early morning to secure boat arrangements with rangers who know the quietest beaches and best snorkeling spots before tour groups arrive from Ao Nang.

attractions.name: ATV Nature View point
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint accessible by ATV offers sweeping panoramas across Krabi's limestone karsts, turquoise Andaman waters, and the scattered islands of the Four Islands group. The adventure ride through jungle trails combined with picture-perfect coastal vistas makes it popular with photography enthusiasts.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวเอทีวี
attractions.tip: Visit during the golden hour before sunset when the light hits the limestone cliffs perfectly and the heat is less intense for the ATV ride up.

attractions.name: The Mud Crabs Sculpture
attractions.description: This oversized sculpture of two fighting mud crabs marks the entrance to Ao Nang Beach, celebrating the region's famous seafood and serving as a quirky landmark. It's the perfect photo opportunity before heading to the beach, with the limestone cliffs creating a dramatic backdrop.
attractions.local_name: ประติมากรรมปูทะเล
attractions.tip: Visit early morning or late afternoon when the light is softer and fewer tourists are around, making it easier to get unobstructed photos with the sculpture.

attractions.name: THAI'D UP ADVENTURES
attractions.description: This adventure outfit runs rock climbing, kayaking, and island-hopping tours from Krabi Town, led by experienced local guides who know the limestone karsts and hidden lagoons intimately. The combination of professional instruction and access to less-crowded climbing spots makes it perfect for both beginners and experienced adventurers.
attractions.local_name: ไทด์ อัพ แอดเวนเจอร์ส
attractions.tip: Book the early morning rock climbing session at Railay to avoid the heat and crowds—guides will teach you technique on beginner-friendly routes before the day-trippers arrive.

attractions.name: Jungle Bar Krabi Town
attractions.description: This lush open-air bar transforms Krabi Town's urban landscape with jungle-themed décor, hanging plants, and quirky photo corners that draw social media enthusiasts. The creative tropical setting paired with affordable cocktails makes it a refreshing evening hangout spot away from beach crowds.
attractions.local_name: Jungle Bar กระบี่
attractions.tip: Visit after 6pm when the fairy lights come on and the temperature cools down—the ambiance is far better than during hot daylight hours.

attractions.name: Wat Kaew Korawaram
attractions.description: This Buddhist temple in the heart of Krabi Town features intricate murals depicting Buddhist hell realms and colorful naga serpents guarding the entrance. The peaceful atmosphere and detailed artwork make it a rewarding stop for those interested in Thai religious art and local spiritual life.
attractions.local_name: วัดแก้วโกราวาส
attractions.tip: Visit the small museum inside the main hall to see ancient artifacts and learn about the temple's history. Dress modestly with shoulders and knees covered to enter the prayer hall.

attractions.name: Wat Sai Thai
attractions.description: This serene riverside temple in Krabi Town features traditional Thai architecture with intricately carved wooden details and a peaceful meditation hall surrounded by mature trees. The temple offers an authentic glimpse into local Buddhist practices away from tourist crowds.
attractions.local_name: วัดไทรใหญ่
attractions.tip: Visit during early morning alms-giving ceremonies around 6-7am to witness monks collecting offerings from local residents, a meaningful cultural experience rarely seen by visitors.

attractions.name: Andaman Cultural Study Center
attractions.description: Gallery & cultural center featuring offbeat artwork & sculpture, plus a beads museum.

attractions.name: Krabi Urban Forest
attractions.description: This green sanctuary in the heart of Krabi Town features elevated boardwalks weaving through mangrove forests and limestone karst formations. The peaceful canopy walkways offer an escape from urban heat while staying minutes from downtown.
attractions.local_name: ป่าในเมืองกระบี่
attractions.tip: Visit early morning around 6-7am to spot monitor lizards and tropical birds when the forest is most active and temperatures are coolest.

attractions.name: Thara Park
attractions.description: Playground, exercise areas & places for sports such as tennis & soccer in a leafy riverfront park.

food.name: Kodam Kitchen branch 1
food.where: 228 หมู่ที่ 3 Thanon Khlong Hang, Tambon Ao Nang, Amphoe Mueang Krabi, Chang Wat Krabi 81180, Thailand
food.tip: Try the morning khao man gai for authentic local flavor. Best visited before 11am when portions are freshest.
food.local_name: โคดัมครัว สาขา 1

food.name: Bamboo Beach Club Krabi
food.where: 32, 14, Tambon Ao Nang, Amphoe Mueang Krabi, Chang Wat Krabi 81180, Thailand
food.tip: Visit at sunset for stunning beach views with cocktails. Try their signature tropical drinks and fresh seafood platters.
food.local_name: บ้านไผ่บีชคลับ กระบี่

food.name: GAIA Kitchen & Bar
food.where: 88, Tambon Ao Nang, Amphoe Mueang Krabi, Chang Wat Krabi 81180, Thailand
food.tip: Try their sunset cocktails on the beachfront terrace. Best visited during golden hour for views and ambiance.
food.local_name: ไกอา คิชเchen แอนด์ บาร์

food.name: Lae Lay Grill
food.where: 89 หมู่ 3 อ่าวนาง Tambon Ao Nang, Amphoe Mueang Krabi, Chang Wat Krabi 81180, Thailand
food.tip: Reserve a beachfront table at sunset for stunning Andaman Sea views. Fresh seafood grilled to perfection.
food.local_name: แล่ เลย์ กริลล์

food.name: Kiran Kitchen Restaurant - Ao Nang Beach
food.where: Center Point, Tambon Ao Nang, beach, Chang Wat Krabi 81180, Thailand
food.tip: Order the curry dishes at sunset while overlooking the beach. Best value lunch specials before 2pm daily.
food.local_name: ครัวกิรณ์ ร้านอาหาร อ่าวนาง บีช

food.name: The Hilltop Ao Nang
food.where: 99 หมู่ที่ 3 Tambon Ao Nang, Amphoe Mueang Krabi, Chang Wat Krabi 81000, Thailand
food.tip: Elevated restaurant & bar offering familiar Thai cuisine & outdoor tables with sweeping vistas.

food.name: REEVE Beach Club Krabi
food.where: 31, 1, Tambon Ao Nang, Amphoe Mueang Krabi, Chang Wat Krabi 81180, Thailand
food.tip: Arrive at sunset for beachfront views. Fresh seafood grilled daily; try the sea bass with garlic and chili.
food.local_name: รีฟ บีช คลับ กระบี่

food.name: THAI ME UP PANG Restaurant and Bar
food.where: 206 Moo2 Beach Road, Tambon Ao Nang, Amphoe Mueang Krabi, Chang Wat Krabi 81180, Thailand
food.tip: Order the pad thai and fresh spring rolls during sunset for best views at Ao Nang Beach location.
food.local_name: ไทยมีอัพแพง เรสเตอรองและบาร์

food.name: Jungle Kitchen
food.where: 1052 Tambon Ao Nang, เมือง Chang Wat Krabi 81110, Thailand
food.tip: Order the jungle curry with fresh local herbs—best enjoyed at sunset with a cold Thai beer.
food.local_name: จังเกิลคิचเชน

food.name: THE COFFEE CLUB - Ao Nang
food.where: 273 Moo 2, Ao Nang, Muang Krabi, Aonang, ตำบล อ่าวนาง Krabi, กระบี่ 81000, Thailand
food.tip: Order their signature iced coffee and pastries in the morning before the heat peaks. Great wifi for remote work.
food.local_name: เดอะ คอฟฟี่ คลับ - อ่าวนาง

food.name: Café 8.98 Ao Nang
food.where: อําเภอ, 143/7-8, Tambon Ao Nang, Amphoe Mueang Krabi, Chang Wat Krabi 81180, Thailand
food.tip: Visit early morning for fresh pastries and peaceful sea views. Their specialty coffee pairs perfectly with homemade desserts.

koh_samui.name: Koh Samui
koh_samui.tagline: Gulf Island of Coconut Palms and Full Moon Parties

attractions.name: LAVALED SAMUI (Chaweng Walking Arcade)
attractions.description: This vibrant open-air arcade in Chaweng features colorful shophouses, neon-lit alleyways, and creative art installations designed for photos. It's the perfect spot to capture eye-catching content while browsing local boutiques and cafes in a retro-modern Thai setting.
attractions.local_name: ลาวาเลด สมุย
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon golden hour for the best natural lighting before the neon signs illuminate at dusk, giving you two distinct photo opportunities in one visit.

attractions.name: Easykart.net โกคาร์ท Go-Karting (Ko Samui, Chaweng Lake)
attractions.description: This outdoor go-kart track near Chaweng Lake features a 600-meter circuit winding through tropical surroundings with tight corners and straightaways that challenge both beginners and experienced drivers. Racing here offers an adrenaline rush away from the beach, with well-maintained karts and a family-friendly atmosphere perfect for friendly competitions.
attractions.local_name: อีซี่คาร์ท โกคาร์ท
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon when temperatures drop and the track is less crowded. Book multiple sessions for better rates, and wear closed-toe shoes—they won't let you race in flip-flops.

attractions.name: Chaweng Beach
attractions.description: Koh Samui's longest stretch of powdery white sand curves for 7 kilometers, backed by swaying palms and lined with beach clubs, restaurants, and water sports operators. The crystal-clear turquoise waters and lively atmosphere make it the island's most popular beach destination.
attractions.local_name: หาดเฉวง
attractions.tip: Visit the northern end near Chaweng Noi for calmer waters and fewer crowds, especially during high season when the main central stretch gets packed with tourists and jet skis.

attractions.name: Secret Garden Beach Resort
attractions.description: Laid-back rooms & bungalows in a down-to-earth resort offering a restaurant & a beachfront bar.

attractions.name: Day & Night of Koh Samui
attractions.description: This photography-focused experience captures Koh Samui's transformation from sun-drenched beaches to neon-lit nightlife through guided photo walks around Chaweng. The curated route takes you to prime vantage points most tourists miss, perfect for building your travel portfolio.
attractions.local_name: เดย์แอนด์ไนท์ออฟเกาะสมุย
attractions.tip: Join the sunset session starting at 5pm to capture both golden hour beach shots and the transition into Chaweng's vibrant night scene in one tour.

attractions.name: ARKbar Beach Club & Resort
attractions.description: Bustling beachfront resort offering a nightclub & 2 restaurants, plus pools with swim-up bars.

attractions.name: THE COCOON - SAMUI VIEWPOINT
attractions.description: This distinctive white cocoon-shaped structure perches on a hillside above Chaweng, offering sweeping views across the turquoise bay and palm-fringed coastline. The architectural design creates perfect frames for photos while providing shade and a comfortable spot to take in the island's eastern panorama.
attractions.local_name: เดอะโคคูน จุดชมวิว เกาะสมุย
attractions.tip: Visit around 4-5pm when the afternoon light is golden but not too harsh, and the crowds are lighter than midday. Park at the base and walk up the short path.

attractions.name: Fisherman's Village Night Market
attractions.description: This bustling Friday evening market in Bophut transforms a quiet fishing village street into a vibrant pedestrian zone lined with wooden shophouses, live music stages, and open-air seafood grills. The authentic local atmosphere combined with quality handicrafts and street food makes it Koh Samui's most atmospheric night market experience.
attractions.local_name: ตลาดชาวประมงบ้านบางรัก
attractions.tip: Arrive around 6pm before the crowds to snag the best street food deals and browse handicraft stalls comfortably. The grilled seafood at the beachfront end offers better value than similar stalls in Chaweng.

attractions.name: Wat Plai Laem
attractions.description: Tranquil, colorful temple complex featuring a lake with an 18-armed statue of the goddess Guanyin.

attractions.name: เจดีย์​ศรีสุคตคีรี (เจดีย์เขาหัวจุก)
attractions.description: This golden hilltop chedi rises from Khao Hua Jook mountain, offering panoramic views across Chaweng Beach and the Gulf of Thailand through traditional Thai architecture. The peaceful temple grounds and expansive coastal vistas make it perfect for catching the afternoon light over the turquoise waters below.
attractions.local_name: เจดีย์ศรีสุคตคีรี (เจดีย์เขาหัวจุก)
attractions.tip: Visit in late afternoon when the golden chedi glows in warm light and temperatures are cooler for the uphill walk. Wear modest clothing covering shoulders and knees as this is an active religious site.

attractions.name: Wat Phra Yai
attractions.description: Massive, circa-1972 gilded Buddha statue at the colorful Phra Yai temple complex.

attractions.name: Statue of the goddess Guanyin
attractions.description: This towering white Guanyin statue stands gracefully overlooking the coastline near Chaweng Beach, radiating serenity with intricate Buddhist craftsmanship and peaceful temple grounds. Visit for a moment of tranquility away from the beach crowds, combined with panoramic views of Koh Samui's eastern shore.
attractions.local_name: พระโพธิสัตว์กวนอิม
attractions.tip: Come early morning around 7-8am to avoid tour groups and experience the peaceful atmosphere with cooler temperatures, perfect for photography in soft natural light.

attractions.name: Mae Nam Viewpoint
attractions.description: Perched on a hillside between Lamai and Mae Nam, this viewpoint offers sweeping panoramas across Koh Samui's northeastern coastline and the Gulf of Thailand. The elevated vantage point captures the contrast between turquoise waters, coconut groves, and the island's lush interior.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวแม่น้ำ
attractions.tip: Visit during the golden hour before sunset when the light is softer and the crowds are thinner than midday, making for better photo opportunities without harsh shadows.

attractions.name: Bophut Fisherman Village View Point
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint overlooks the traditional wooden shophouses and turquoise waters of Bophut's charming fishing village, framing the perfect shot of old Samui's coastal character. The perspective captures both the rustic village rooftops and Fisherman's Wharf stretching toward the sea, ideal for golden hour photography.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวหมู่บ้านชาวประมงบ่อผุด
attractions.tip: Arrive 30 minutes before sunset to secure the best angle and catch the warm light washing over the red-roofed village buildings and boats below.

attractions.name: The Wharf Walking Street
attractions.description: This open-air boardwalk shopping street stretches along the waterfront with colorful wooden shophouses selling beachwear, handicrafts, and souvenirs alongside cozy cafes and bars. The relaxed atmosphere and direct beach access make it perfect for a leisurely evening stroll combined with casual dining and browsing.
attractions.local_name: เดอะ วาร์ฟ วอล์คกิ้ง สตรีท
attractions.tip: Visit on Friday evenings when the adjacent Fisherman's Village Walking Street market is in full swing, then walk over to The Wharf for a quieter dining experience with better seating availability.

attractions.name: The Villager Fisherman's Village
attractions.description: This charming waterfront development in Bophut captures the essence of a traditional Thai fishing village with wooden shophouses hosting boutiques, galleries, and seafood restaurants. The relaxed atmosphere combined with beachfront dining makes it perfect for a leisurely afternoon stroll and fresh catch dinner.
attractions.local_name: หมู่บ้านชาวประมงเดอะวิลเลจเจอร์
attractions.tip: Visit on Friday evenings when the famous Fisherman's Village Walking Street market takes over, offering street food, handmade crafts, and live music from 5pm onwards.

attractions.name: Coco Tam’s
attractions.description: A colorful beachfront bar and restaurant in Fisherman's Village known for its quirky decor, lanterns, and laid-back atmosphere right on Bophut Beach. The perfect spot to sink your toes in the sand while enjoying sunset cocktails and fresh seafood with live music setting the mood.
attractions.local_name: โคโค่ แทมส์
attractions.tip: Arrive around 5:30 PM to snag one of the beanbag seats directly on the sand before the sunset crowd fills in—the best spots go quickly.

attractions.name: Wild Child Organic Kitchen & Elephant Sanctuary Experience
attractions.description: This intimate sanctuary lets you feed, bathe, and walk alongside rescued elephants in their natural habitat, followed by an organic farm-to-table meal prepared with ingredients grown on-site. The chance to observe these gentle giants up close in an ethical, non-riding environment while supporting genuine elephant welfare makes this experience meaningful.
attractions.local_name: Wild Child Organic Kitchen & Elephant Sanctuary Experience
attractions.tip: Book the morning session to see elephants at their most active during feeding time, and wear clothes you don't mind getting muddy since the mud bath portion is hands-on and messy.

attractions.name: Greenlight Fisherman's Village Resort & Cafe
attractions.description: This laid-back beachfront cafe and resort sits along Bophut's famous Fisherman's Village walking street, offering direct sea views and casual Thai-Western fusion dining. The photogenic wooden deck overlooking the Gulf of Thailand makes it a popular spot for sunset drinks and relaxed meals away from the busier beach clubs.
attractions.local_name: กรีนไลท์ ฟิชเชอร์แมนส์ วิลเลจ รีสอร์ท แอนด์ คาเฟ่
attractions.tip: Visit during weekday afternoons for the quietest atmosphere and best table selection on the sea-facing deck before the evening crowds arrive from the walking street.

attractions.name: Garnish.Samui
attractions.description: This creative design space in Bophut showcases contemporary tropical aesthetics through carefully curated installations and photo-friendly corners. The perfect blend of art gallery meets lifestyle concept store makes it a refreshing cultural pause between beach days.
attractions.local_name: Garnish.Samui
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon when natural light filters through the space beautifully for photos, and the midday heat has passed for more comfortable browsing.

attractions.name: ZOI
attractions.description: This boutique creative space in Bophut showcases contemporary art installations and curated design pieces perfect for unique photo opportunities. The thoughtfully arranged aesthetic corners and artistic backdrops make it Koh Samui's rising star for social media content.
attractions.local_name: ZOI
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon when natural light filters through the space beautifully. The owners occasionally rotate installations, so check their social media before visiting to see current displays.

attractions.name: Red Moon
attractions.description: This photogenic art installation features a giant glowing red moon structure set against Fisherman's Village backdrop, creating dramatic photo opportunities day and night. The perfect spot to capture that otherworldly shot for your social media feed with minimal crowds.
attractions.local_name: เรดมูน
attractions.tip: Visit during blue hour just after sunset when the red moon is illuminated but natural light remains—you'll get the most balanced and dramatic photos without harsh shadows.

attractions.name: Villa Chang - Fisherman's village Bophut
attractions.description: This charming guesthouse sits in the heart of Fisherman's Village, offering a prime vantage point to observe the area's transformation from sleepy fishing port to boutique destination. The perfect base for exploring Bophut's Walking Street market and beachfront dining scene just steps away.
attractions.local_name: วิลล่าช้าง - หมู่บ้านชาวประมงบ่อผุด
attractions.tip: Visit on Friday evenings when the Walking Street market comes alive—you'll be staying close enough to return to Villa Chang between browsing sessions without losing your energy.

attractions.name: Samui Paradise Bayfront
attractions.description: This waterfront spot along Bophut's coastline offers unobstructed views across the bay with palm-fringed shores and calm turquoise waters perfect for easy swimming. The peaceful setting and natural beauty make it ideal for relaxation away from crowded beach clubs.
attractions.local_name: สมุยพาราไดซ์เบย์ฟร้อนท์
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon when the heat subsides and the light becomes softer for photography, with fewer visitors around than midday hours.

attractions.name: Hidden Hut Samui
attractions.description: This tucked-away jungle retreat offers handcrafted bamboo swings, quirky photo installations, and cozy nooks hidden among tropical foliage in the Big Buddha area. The intimate, artist-designed spaces create perfectly framed shots without the crowds of mainstream Instagram spots.
attractions.local_name: Hidden Hut Samui
attractions.tip: Visit during the golden hour before sunset when natural light filters through the trees beautifully. Weekday mornings are quietest if you want the installations to yourself.

attractions.name: The Sunset
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint near Big Buddha temple captures the sun melting into the Gulf of Thailand, painting the sky in brilliant oranges and purples above Koh Phangan's silhouette. The unobstructed western vista makes it one of the island's most reliable spots for dramatic sunset photography.
attractions.local_name: พระอาทิตย์ตก
attractions.tip: Arrive 30 minutes before sunset to secure a good spot along the viewing area. The light is best for photography when the sun is halfway down.

attractions.name: Treehouse Silent Beach
attractions.description: Simple huts & colorful bungalows on a casual beachfront property offering a restaurant/bar.

attractions.name: Samui exotic park
attractions.description: This compact animal park on Koh Samui's west coast features interactive animal shows with monkeys, elephants, snakes, and birds performing tricks and demonstrations. The main draw is the opportunity to get close-up photos with exotic animals including pythons, gibbons, and colorful parrots in a casual tropical garden setting.
attractions.local_name: สวนสัตว์สมุยเอ็กโซติก
attractions.tip: Arrive early for the first show at 10:30am when animals are most energetic and crowds are smaller, giving you better photo opportunities and closer interaction with the handlers.

attractions.name: Samui Elephant Kingdom Sanctuary
attractions.description: This ethical elephant sanctuary in Koh Samui's lush interior lets visitors feed, bathe, and walk alongside rescued elephants in their natural habitat without riding them. The chance to interact respectfully with these gentle giants while learning about their conservation makes it a meaningful wildlife encounter.
attractions.local_name: อาณาจักรช้างสมุย
attractions.tip: Book the morning session to avoid the midday heat and see the elephants at their most active during feeding and bathing time in the mud pits.

attractions.name: Tarnim Magic Garden
attractions.description: Lush hilltop garden flanked by waterfalls & displays of statues of the Buddha, animals & more.

attractions.name: Hin Ta Hin Yai
attractions.description: Storied natural rock formations known for bearing a resemblance to male & female genitalia.

attractions.name: Lamai View Point & Heart Stone
attractions.description: Lookout deck with a bar, on a forested hill reached by trail or cable car, with views to the sea.

attractions.name: Wat Khao Chedi
attractions.description: This hilltop temple features a striking white chedi perched on a rocky outcrop with sweeping views over Chaweng Beach and the Gulf of Thailand. The temple's elevated position and ornate Buddhist architecture offer both spiritual contemplation and exceptional photo opportunities.
attractions.local_name: วัดเขาเจดีย์
attractions.tip: Visit early morning around 7-8am to see monks during their routines and avoid the midday heat on the steep climb up.

attractions.name: Wat Khunaram (Phra Wihan Luang Pho Daeng)
attractions.description: Small, busy temple with Buddhist art, best known for the mummified monk on display.

attractions.name: Wat Ratchathammaram Red Temple
attractions.description: Terracotta-hued Buddhist temple richly decorated with sculptures of sea monsters & warriors.

attractions.name: Wat Phra Chedi Laem Sor
attractions.description: Small, golden pagoda with ornate details in a serene oceanfront setting with a sandy beach.

attractions.name: Chedi Phra That Sila Ngu
attractions.description: This hilltop temple complex features a striking white pagoda and a massive golden Buddha statue overlooking Chaweng Bay, set against lush tropical hillside gardens. The panoramic views across Koh Samui's east coast from the elevated platform reward the climb up the naga-lined staircase.
attractions.local_name: เจดีย์พระธาตุศิลางู
attractions.tip: Visit in late afternoon around 4-5pm when tour groups have left and the softer light creates better photos of the golden Buddha without harsh shadows.

attractions.name: Sun Star Viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint perched on a hillside north of Chaweng offers sweeping panoramas across the turquoise bay, framed by swaying palms and rocky outcrops. The open-air platform captures both sunrise and sunset angles, making it a photographer's dream without the crowds of busier lookouts.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวซันสตาร์
attractions.tip: Arrive 30 minutes before sunset when the light is golden and temperatures cool down. The access road is steep, so rent a scooter with good brakes or take a taxi.

attractions.name: Hua Thanon View Point
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint along the coastal road between Lamai and Hua Thanon village offers sweeping views of turquoise bays framed by traditional fishing boats and palm-lined shores. The unobstructed panoramas capture Koh Samui's southeastern coastline without the crowds found at more popular spots.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวหัวถนน
attractions.tip: Visit in late afternoon when fishing boats return to shore and the light softens for photography. Park safely off the narrow road and watch for motorbikes.

attractions.name: Samui Elephant Home
attractions.description: This ethical sanctuary lets you observe rescued elephants bathing, foraging, and roaming freely in their natural jungle habitat without riding or performances. The chance to feed and interact with these gentle giants at eye level in an environment that prioritizes their wellbeing makes every visit deeply meaningful.
attractions.local_name: สมุยเอเลเฟ่นท์โฮม
attractions.tip: Book the morning session when elephants are most active and playful during their mud bath. Wear clothes you don't mind getting dirty and bring a change of shoes.

attractions.name: Teddy Weed Beach
attractions.description: This intimate rocky cove sits tucked between Lamai's larger beaches, featuring smooth granite boulders that frame shallow turquoise pools perfect for wading. The dramatic rock formations create countless photo angles that capture Koh Samui's natural coastal beauty without the crowds.
attractions.local_name: หาดเท็ดดี้วีด
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide when the water recedes to reveal calm, knee-deep pools between the rocks—ideal for photographing reflections and exploring small marine life.

attractions.name: Lamai Beach
attractions.description: This golden-sand beach on Koh Samui's southeastern coast stretches over two kilometers, offering calmer waves than Chaweng and a more laid-back atmosphere with local seafood restaurants lining the shore. The shallow, clear waters and relaxed vibe make it perfect for families seeking a peaceful beach day without the party crowds.
attractions.local_name: หาดละไม
attractions.tip: Visit the southern end near Hin Ta and Hin Yai rocks early morning for the best photos and fewer tourists, then grab fresh coconut ice cream from beachside vendors.

attractions.name: House of Suzy
attractions.description: House of Suzy is a whimsical art installation featuring colorful, oversized sculptures and interactive photo setups scattered across a tropical garden setting in Lamai. The playful, surreal installations—from giant swings to quirky statues—create endless photo opportunities perfect for social media enthusiasts.
attractions.local_name: เฮาส์ออฟซูซี่
attractions.tip: Visit on weekday mornings to avoid crowds and have the installations to yourself for uninterrupted photo sessions with better natural lighting.

attractions.name: Walk on the Rock
attractions.description: A dramatic natural rock formation jutting into the turquoise waters of Lamai Beach, featuring flat surfaces perfect for walking and climbing along the coastline. The unique geological formations create natural pools and viewpoints that frame the entire bay in ways you won't find at busier beaches.
attractions.local_name: เดินบนหิน
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide in the late afternoon when the rocks are easier to navigate and the lighting is perfect for photos without harsh shadows.

attractions.name: Tiki Beach Club and Bungalows Samui
attractions.description: This Polynesian-themed beach club on Lamai Beach features tropical cocktails, bamboo bungalows, and laid-back reggae vibes with direct sand access. The photogenic tiki statues, colorful beach loungers, and sunset drinks make it a perfect spot for relaxed beachfront socializing.
attractions.local_name: ทิกิ บีช คลับ แอนด์ บังกะโลว์ สมุย
attractions.tip: Arrive in late afternoon to claim a beanbag on the sand before sunset, when the golden hour light creates the best photos and the beach club atmosphere peaks.

attractions.name: The Third Day Beach Club
attractions.description: This stylish beach club on Lamai Beach combines a relaxed infinity pool, daybeds overlooking turquoise waters, and a menu of Mediterranean-inspired dishes with fresh seafood. The sunset views paired with creative cocktails create the perfect backdrop for a leisurely afternoon by the sea.
attractions.local_name: เดอะเธิร์ดเดย์บีชคลับ
attractions.tip: Arrive before 2pm on weekends to secure a prime poolside daybed, as walk-ins fill up quickly during high season.

attractions.name: Wat Lamai
attractions.description: This traditional Buddhist temple in Lamai features a striking golden chedi and intricate murals depicting local folklore and Buddhist teachings. The temple grounds offer a peaceful retreat where you can observe monks during their daily routines and experience authentic island spirituality away from the beach crowds.
attractions.local_name: วัดละไม
attractions.tip: Visit during morning alms-giving ceremonies around 7am to see the monks receive offerings from locals, providing an authentic glimpse into daily Buddhist practice rarely seen by tourists.

attractions.name: เจดีย์แหลมสอ
attractions.description: This twin pagoda stands dramatically on a rocky peninsula jutting into the turquoise Gulf of Thailand, surrounded by crashing waves and panoramic coastal views. The photogenic golden spires against the sea backdrop create one of Lamai Beach's most striking visual compositions.
attractions.local_name: เจดีย์แหลมสอ
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide in the late afternoon when you can walk around the rocky base more easily and capture the pagodas with golden hour lighting against the ocean.

attractions.name: Paradise Park​ Farm
attractions.description: Mountaintop park featuring a pool with panoramic views, a cafe & animals, including colored pigeons.

attractions.name: Buddha's Footprint
attractions.description: A sacred golden footprint of Buddha sits atop a hillside platform near Big Buddha, drawing Buddhist pilgrims and visitors seeking merit-making. The elevated position offers sweeping views across northern Koh Samui's coastline and neighboring islands.
attractions.local_name: รอยพระพุทธบาท
attractions.tip: Visit early morning before 9am to avoid heat and tour groups. Locals leave small gold leaf squares here—you can purchase some at the base to participate in this merit-making tradition.

attractions.name: Na Mueang viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint offers sweeping panoramas across Koh Samui's lush interior valleys, emerald jungle canopy, and glimpses of the island's coastline stretching toward the horizon. The refreshingly uncrowded platform provides perfect conditions for photography during golden hour when the tropical light softens across the mountainous terrain.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวน้ำเมือง
attractions.tip: Arrive in late afternoon when tour groups have left and the angled sunlight creates dramatic shadows across the valley, making photos far more compelling than harsh midday shots.

attractions.name: Na Muang 2 Waterfall
attractions.description: This 80-meter cascade plunges down purple-hued rocks into a natural pool surrounded by dense jungle canopy, accessible via a steep 30-minute trek from the parking area. The relatively challenging hike keeps crowds thinner than its sister waterfall, rewarding visitors with a more secluded swimming spot.
attractions.local_name: น้ำตกหน้าเมือง 2
attractions.tip: Wear proper hiking shoes with grip as the trail becomes slippery near the falls. Visit during rainy season for stronger flow, but check conditions first as the path can become treacherous.

attractions.name: Suan Sam Anong
attractions.description: This lush hillside garden showcases tropical fruit trees, ornamental plants, and traditional Thai landscaping with sweeping views over Nathon Bay. The peaceful terraced grounds offer a genuine escape into nature without the crowds found at more commercial Koh Samui attractions.
attractions.local_name: สวนสามอนงค์
attractions.tip: Visit early morning when the garden is cooler and the light is perfect for photography. The caretaker sometimes offers fresh fruit samples from trees in season.

attractions.name: Lad Koh View Point
attractions.description: This coastal stop offers panoramic seaside views & has a small shaded pavilion with seating.

attractions.name: The Jungle Club
attractions.description: Casual hillside resort offering gulf views, plus an infinity pool & an open-air restaurant/bar.

attractions.name: Samui Sunshine Bar
attractions.description: This beachfront bar on Chaweng Beach serves colorful cocktails and Thai craft beers with tables set right on the sand, perfect for sunset sessions. The laid-back atmosphere and direct beach access make it ideal for cooling down between swims with a drink in hand.
attractions.local_name: ซามุย ซันชายน์ บาร์
attractions.tip: Arrive around 5pm to claim a prime beachfront table before the sunset crowd arrives, and ask the bartender to recommend their daily special cocktails made with fresh local fruits.

attractions.name: Jungle Road Viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated roadside viewpoint along the winding jungle route offers sweeping views across Chaweng Beach's turquoise coastline and the island's lush interior hills. The perfect spot to capture the dramatic contrast between Koh Samui's emerald forests and azure waters in a single frame.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวจังเกิ้ลโรด
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon around 4-5 PM when the light is softer for photography and the heat less intense. Park carefully on the narrow shoulder.

attractions.name: Panoramic View Samui
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint overlooks Fisherman's Village and the northern coastline, offering sweeping vistas of Bophut Bay and neighboring islands. The peaceful, uncrowded setting makes it ideal for photography during golden hour when the light transforms the seascape.
attractions.local_name: พาโนรามิค วิว สมุย
attractions.tip: Arrive 45 minutes before sunset for the best light and fewer visitors. The unpaved access road can be rough, so drive slowly or park below and walk up.

attractions.name: Treetop Tour Zipline
attractions.description: Soar through the jungle canopy on a network of ziplines that offer aerial views of Koh Samui's lush tropical forest and distant coastal glimpses. The combination of adrenaline-pumping speed and close encounters with wildlife makes this an unforgettable way to experience the island's natural beauty.
attractions.local_name: ทรีท็อป ทัวร์ ซิปไลน์
attractions.tip: Book the earliest morning slot to avoid crowds and catch wildlife when animals are most active. Wear closed-toe shoes and leave valuables behind as lockers aren't always available.

attractions.name: Pra Buddha Dīpankara
attractions.description: This bronze Buddha statue depicts Dipankara, the Buddha who prophesied Gautama's enlightenment, captured in a walking posture with intricate flame motifs symbolizing his name meaning 'the lamp bearer.' The serene statue offers a quieter, more contemplative counterpoint to the nearby Big Buddha, perfect for understanding Buddhist cosmology beyond the main tourist circuit.
attractions.local_name: พระพุทธทีปังกร
attractions.tip: Visit during early morning when monks from nearby temples sometimes come to pay respects, offering a chance to observe genuine devotional practices rather than tourist crowds.

attractions.name: Mu Ko Ang Thong National Marine Park
attractions.description: National park over an island archipelago featuring forested peaks, scenic views & abundant wildlife.

attractions.name: Viewpoint beach and ocean
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint offers unobstructed panoramas of Chaweng Beach's turquoise waters stretching toward the horizon, with fringing palm trees dotting the coastline below. The peaceful setting away from the beach crowds makes it perfect for capturing the full sweep of Koh Samui's eastern shoreline.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวชายหาดและมหาสมุทร
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon around 4-5pm when the harsh midday sun softens and the light brings out the ocean's blue-green hues without the intense glare.

attractions.name: Lay Lagom Cafe’Bar
attractions.description: This Scandinavian-inspired cafe bar on Lamai Beach serves specialty coffee and craft cocktails in a minimalist setting with white-washed walls and natural wood accents. The photogenic interior design and creative drink presentations make it a favorite spot for capturing that perfect beach-holiday aesthetic.
attractions.local_name: Lay Lagom Cafe'Bar
attractions.tip: Visit during golden hour for the best natural lighting through their large windows, and order the signature iced latte which comes in a unique glassware that photographs beautifully.

attractions.name: Hin Lat Waterfall
attractions.description: Secluded waterfall amid trees & jungle foliage with swimming & a nearby elephant reserve.

attractions.name: Nathon Pier
attractions.description: Major ferry pier serving Koh Samui's northwest, with a number of shops & food stalls around.

attractions.name: Taling ngam beach
attractions.description: This peaceful crescent of golden sand on Koh Samui's southwestern coast offers spectacular sunset views over the Five Islands archipelago, with calm waters perfect for swimming. The quieter, less developed atmosphere compared to Chaweng or Lamai makes it ideal for those seeking a tranquil beach escape.
attractions.local_name: หาดตลิ่งงาม
attractions.tip: Visit during low tide in late afternoon when the shallow waters create natural pools and the exposed sandbar extends toward the islands, perfect for sunset photography without crowds.

attractions.name: Nathon Hainan Shrine
attractions.description: This colorful Chinese-Thai shrine in Nathon's old quarter honors Hainan islanders who migrated to Koh Samui generations ago, with intricate dragon carvings and traditional red-and-gold architecture. The peaceful courtyard offers a glimpse into the island's multicultural heritage away from the beach crowds.
attractions.local_name: ศาลเจ้าไหหลำนาธร
attractions.tip: Visit during morning hours when local devotees bring fresh fruit offerings and burn incense—it's the most authentic time to witness living traditions and photograph the shrine without crowds.

attractions.name: Nathon Old Town
attractions.description: Nathon's charming old town reveals weathered Sino-Portuguese shophouses along quiet streets where local fishermen still moor their boats at the working pier. This is Koh Samui's original port town, offering an authentic glimpse into island life before tourism transformed the eastern beaches.
attractions.local_name: เมืองเก่านาธอน
attractions.tip: Visit the fresh market near the pier early morning for local breakfast like khao tom and to see fishermen unloading their catch before it heads to island restaurants.

attractions.name: Nathon Samui Hall of Historical
attractions.description: This compact heritage museum in Nathon showcases Koh Samui's fishing village past through old photographs, traditional tools, and artifacts from the island's Chinese-Thai community. The carefully curated collection offers a rare glimpse into pre-tourism Samui that most visitors never see.
attractions.local_name: หอประวัติศาสตร์หน้าทอนสมุย
attractions.tip: Visit during weekday mornings when staff are more available to share stories about the exhibits and old Nathon families who donated the historical items.

attractions.name: Nathon Sunset Viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint on Nathon's western edge offers unobstructed views across the Gulf of Thailand, with the sun sinking directly into the horizon behind distant islands. The lack of crowds and easy roadside access make it perfect for a quick, peaceful sunset stop while exploring the town.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวพระอาทิตย์ตกนาธอน
attractions.tip: Arrive 20 minutes before sunset and park along the small pull-off area. Locals often gather at the adjacent temple grounds for a quieter vantage point with stone benches.

attractions.name: Sunset view point
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint along Nathon's coastal road offers unobstructed views across the Gulf of Thailand toward the mainland, with vibrant orange and pink skies reflecting off the water. Arrive with plenty of time to watch the sun slowly dip below the horizon while local fishermen return to shore below.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมพระอัตตกาลัง
attractions.tip: Come 30 minutes before sunset to secure the best spot and enjoy the changing light. Bring mosquito repellent as the coastal breeze attracts insects during golden hour.

attractions.name: Phu Jun Jaras Viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint on Koh Samui's west coast offers sweeping panoramas of the Gulf of Thailand, neighboring islands, and Nathon Bay framed by lush tropical hillsides. The quiet, uncrowded setting makes it perfect for photographers seeking golden hour shots without the tourist masses.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวภูจันจารัส
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon for the best light and cooler temperatures. The dirt road leading up can be steep and rough, so a scooter with good tires or a car is recommended.

attractions.name: Khao Naw(เขานาว)
attractions.description: A lesser-known viewpoint on Koh Samui's west coast offering panoramic vistas over Nathon town, the Gulf of Thailand, and nearby islands. The relatively easy climb rewards visitors with golden hour views and a peaceful escape from the island's busier eastern beaches.
attractions.local_name: เขานาว
attractions.tip: Visit in late afternoon for the best light and clearer skies. The unmarked trail starts behind the telecommunications tower—ask locals in Nathon for directions to avoid getting lost.

attractions.name: Tan Rua Waterfall
attractions.description: This modest jungle waterfall cascades into a refreshing natural pool surrounded by smooth rocks and tropical vegetation. The cool, clear water offers a welcome escape from the island heat, perfect for a quick dip after exploring Chaweng's beaches.
attractions.local_name: น้ำตกตาลเรือ
attractions.tip: Visit during the rainy season (October to December) when water flow is strongest. The trail can be slippery, so wear proper footwear with grip rather than flip-flops.

attractions.name: Samui View Point
attractions.description: Perched on a hillside between Chaweng and Lamai, this viewpoint offers sweeping vistas of turquoise bays, palm-fringed coastlines, and the island's eastern beaches from a convenient roadside stop. Come for the panoramic photo opportunities that capture multiple beaches in a single frame.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวเกาะสมุย
attractions.tip: Visit early morning around 7-8am for the clearest views before haze builds up and to avoid tour groups that arrive mid-morning.

attractions.name: Jo Garden And Viewpoint
attractions.description: This hillside garden on Chaweng's eastern ridge features tropical plants and panoramic views stretching across Chaweng Beach to the Gulf of Thailand. The elevated vantage point offers some of Koh Samui's best sunset photo opportunities without the crowds of more popular viewpoints.
attractions.local_name: โจ การ์เด้น แอนด์ วิวพอยท์
attractions.tip: Visit late afternoon around 4-5pm when the light is golden but before peak sunset crowds arrive. The garden owner sometimes offers fresh coconuts to visitors.

attractions.name: Khun Si Viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint perches on a hillside between Chaweng and Lamai, offering sweeping panoramas across Koh Samui's eastern coastline and the sparkling Gulf of Thailand. The panoramic vista captures both sunrise and sunset angles, making it a versatile spot for photographers seeking that perfect coastal shot.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิวขุนศรี
attractions.tip: Visit during late afternoon when the light softens and fewer tour groups are present. Park carefully on the narrow roadside and watch for motorbikes passing on the steep access road.

attractions.name: View point
attractions.description: This elevated lookout perched above Chaweng Beach captures sweeping panoramas of turquoise waters, white sands, and the curve of Samui's eastern coastline. Visit at golden hour when the sun bathes the entire bay in warm light, perfect for photography.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมวิว
attractions.tip: Arrive 30 minutes before sunset and position yourself on the right side of the viewpoint for unobstructed shots without crowds blocking the frame.

attractions.name: Tree Bridge Zipline Samui
attractions.description: Soar through the jungle canopy on one of Koh Samui's most thrilling zipline courses, featuring a unique tree bridge walkway that connects platforms high above the forest floor. The combination of adrenaline-pumping ziplines and suspended bridge walks offers an immersive aerial adventure through Lamai's lush tropical landscape.
attractions.local_name: ทรี บริดจ์ ซิปไลน์ สมุย
attractions.tip: Book the earliest morning slot to avoid midday heat and enjoy cooler temperatures in the jungle canopy. Wear closed-toe shoes with good grip for the tree bridge sections.
