pai.name: Pai
pai.tagline: Hippie Valley in the Northern Mountains

attractions.name: Pai Walking Street
attractions.description: This car-free street transforms every evening into a vibrant pedestrian market where hill tribe vendors sell handwoven textiles, vintage backpacker tees, and quirky artisan crafts alongside sizzling street food stalls. The laid-back atmosphere and live acoustic music performances make it Pai's social heart where travelers and locals mingle over 20-baht pad thai.
attractions.local_name: ถนนคนเดินปาย
attractions.tip: Arrive around 6pm before crowds peak to snag the best handmade souvenirs from hill tribe artisans, and bring small bills since many vendors don't have change for 500 or 1000 baht notes.

attractions.name: Pai Canyon
attractions.description: Serene, scenic canyon with narrow, ledgy hiking trails that offer sweeping valley views.

attractions.name: Pai Night Market
attractions.description: This lively pedestrian street transforms nightly into a colorful bazaar where local hill tribe vendors sell handmade crafts alongside sizzling food stalls serving northern Thai specialties and fusion dishes. The relaxed atmosphere and affordable prices make it the perfect spot to sample Pai's bohemian culture while browsing unique souvenirs.
attractions.local_name: ตลาดกลางคืนปาย
attractions.tip: Arrive around 6:30 PM to snag the best selection of grilled sausages and fresh spring rolls before the crowds thicken, and bring small bills since many vendors don't carry change.

attractions.name: Pai Adventures
attractions.description: This adventure tour operator specializes in white water rafting on the Pai River, ATV jungle trails, and zip-lining through mountain forests. The white water rafting combines thrilling rapids with peaceful stretches where you can spot local wildlife along the riverbanks.
attractions.local_name: ปาย แอดเวนเจอร์
attractions.tip: Book the morning rafting slot when water levels are higher and the river less crowded, especially during rainy season when rapids become more exciting.

attractions.name: Nature Life @ PAI
attractions.description: This laid-back shop and gallery space showcases handmade crafts, natural products, and quirky bohemian goods created by local artisans in Pai. The eclectic collection of tie-dye clothing, handwoven textiles, and organic skincare makes it a perfect spot to find unique souvenirs.
attractions.local_name: Nature Life @ PAI
attractions.tip: Visit in the late afternoon when the owner is usually around to share stories about the artisans and production methods behind each handmade product.

attractions.name: Tha Pai Hot Spring
attractions.description: Natural hot springs nestled in a lush forest setting where mineral-rich water bubbles up at a perfect soaking temperature. The combination of therapeutic thermal pools and a serene jungle atmosphere makes this an ideal spot to ease tired muscles after trekking or riding the winding roads to Pai.
attractions.local_name: น้ำพุร้อนท่าปาย
attractions.tip: Visit early morning on weekdays to avoid tour groups from Chiang Mai. Bring your own towel and wear a swimsuit you don't mind getting mineral-stained from the spring water.

attractions.name: Jerk Spot Pai
attractions.description: This colourful Caribbean-themed eatery brings authentic Jamaican jerk flavours to Pai's walking street area, with vibrant murals and reggae vibes creating a photogenic tropical escape. The smoky jerk chicken paired with generous portions makes it a favourite among backpackers and food photographers alike.
attractions.local_name: เจิร์ก สปอต ปาย
attractions.tip: Visit during golden hour for the best lighting on the painted exterior walls, and ask for extra jerk sauce on the side if you enjoy serious heat.

attractions.name: ถนนคนเดินเมืองปาย
attractions.description: This evening street market transforms Pai's main road into a pedestrian paradise filled with handicraft stalls, live music, and aromatic street food vendors serving northern Thai specialties. The relaxed bohemian atmosphere perfectly captures Pai's laid-back charm while offering excellent local shopping and people-watching opportunities.
attractions.local_name: ถนนคนเดินเมืองปาย
attractions.tip: Arrive around 6 PM when vendors are still setting up to browse leisurely before crowds arrive, and bring small bills as many stalls don't carry much change.

attractions.name: Sunset Bar
attractions.description: Perched on a hillside overlooking Pai valley, this open-air bar offers sweeping views across rice paddies and mountains while serving cold drinks and simple Thai snacks. The golden hour transforms the entire valley into a painter's palette, making it the ideal spot to watch day turn to night with a Chang beer in hand.
attractions.local_name: ซันเซ็ต บาร์
attractions.tip: Arrive 45 minutes before sunset to claim a good cushion seat on the upper level, where views are unobstructed and you can watch the light change across the valley.

attractions.name: Tha-Pai Memorial Bridge
attractions.description: This vintage steel bridge from World War II spans the Pai River, its weathered beams and wooden planks creating perfect frames for golden-hour photography. The bridge offers unobstructed views of the surrounding mountains and river valley, especially beautiful when morning mist rises from the water.
attractions.local_name: สะพานประวัติศาสตร์ท่าปาย
attractions.tip: Visit at sunrise around 6:30 AM when local farmers cross the bridge and the light is softest, or just before sunset when tour groups have left.

attractions.name: Wat Sri Don Chai
attractions.description: This serene temple sits quietly along Pai's walking street, featuring traditional Lanna-style wooden architecture and a beautifully ornate prayer hall with intricate carvings. The peaceful courtyard offers a calm retreat from the bustling market scene just steps away, perfect for quiet contemplation.
attractions.local_name: วัดศรีดอนชัย
attractions.tip: Visit early morning around 7am to see monks receiving alms from locals and experience the temple at its most authentic before tourist crowds arrive.

attractions.name: Wat Phra That Mae Yen
attractions.description: Temple with a monumental staircase climbing to a huge white Buddha statue & views over Pai.

attractions.name: Vieng Tai Public Park (สวนสาธารณะ เวียงใต้)
attractions.description: This riverside public park features shaded pavilions and walking paths along the Pai River, offering a peaceful retreat from the town center's bustle. The open green spaces and river views make it an ideal spot for picnics and watching local life unfold.
attractions.local_name: สวนสาธารณะเวียงใต้
attractions.tip: Visit in late afternoon when locals gather for exercise and tai chi by the river, giving you an authentic glimpse into daily Pai community life.

attractions.name: I AM PAI
attractions.description: This colorful art installation features oversized letters spelling 'I AM PAI' set against Pai's mountain backdrop, creating the town's most recognizable photo opportunity. Visit for playful Instagram-worthy shots that capture the laid-back creative spirit of this bohemian mountain town.
attractions.local_name: ไอ แอม ปาย
attractions.tip: Arrive at golden hour just before sunset when the light is softest and the colors pop most vibrantly. Fewer tour groups mean you'll have the installation mostly to yourself.

attractions.name: Mae Yen Waterfall
attractions.description: This multi-tiered waterfall cascades through lush jungle about 8 kilometers from Pai town, with a refreshing natural pool at its base perfect for swimming. The relatively easy 20-minute hike through bamboo forest and the cool, clear water make it ideal for escaping Pai's midday heat.
attractions.local_name: น้ำตกแม่เย็น
attractions.tip: Visit during wet season (June-October) for the most impressive water flow, but avoid going immediately after heavy rain when the trail becomes slippery and the current too strong for safe swimming.

attractions.name: Big Buddha Pai
attractions.description: This towering white Buddha statue sits atop a hill overlooking Pai valley, accessible via a steep staircase flanked by dragon balustrades. The panoramic views of the town, mountains, and surrounding rice fields at sunset make the climb worthwhile.
attractions.local_name: พระพุทธรูปใหญ่ ปาย
attractions.tip: Visit in late afternoon around 4-5pm to avoid midday heat and catch golden hour light for photos, but arrive before sunset crowds gather.

attractions.name: Two Huts Pai
attractions.description: Two charming bamboo huts perched on a hillside offer sweeping views over Pai's valley and mountains, creating one of the area's most photogenic spots. The simple setting and peaceful atmosphere make it perfect for catching golden hour while escaping the town's busier viewpoints.
attractions.local_name: ทูฮัทส์ ปาย
attractions.tip: Arrive 45 minutes before sunset to secure the best spot at the huts and watch the light transform across the valley without crowds from the main temple viewpoint.

attractions.name: White Buddha
attractions.description: This gleaming white Buddha statue sits serenely atop a hill overlooking Pai valley, accessible via a steep staircase flanked by naga serpents. The sweeping panoramic views of the patchwork countryside and misty mountains make the climb worthwhile, especially during golden hour.
attractions.local_name: พระพุทธรูปสีขาว
attractions.tip: Visit in late afternoon when tour groups have left and the softer light creates better photos without harsh shadows on the white statue.

attractions.name: Startpoint Mae Yen Waterfall
attractions.description: This multi-tiered waterfall cascades through lush jungle just a short trek from Wat Mae Yen, with refreshing pools perfect for a quick dip. The relatively uncrowded setting and natural rock formations make it an ideal spot to cool off after climbing the temple stairs.
attractions.local_name: น้ำตกแม่เย็น
attractions.tip: Visit during early morning to have the pools mostly to yourself. The trail can be slippery after rain, so wear proper footwear with grip rather than flip-flops.

attractions.name: Huai Nam Dang National Park
attractions.description: Lush national park providing rolling hills with mountain views, waterfalls & hot springs/baths.

attractions.name: Pai Horizon – Sunset Viewpoint & Experience
attractions.description: This hillside viewpoint offers sweeping views over Pai Valley's patchwork of rice fields and mountains, with purpose-built platforms and swings positioned for golden hour photography. Come for the unobstructed 270-degree panorama that turns brilliant shades of orange and pink as the sun dips behind the western mountains.
attractions.local_name: ปายฮอไรซัน
attractions.tip: Arrive 45 minutes before sunset to claim the best swing spots and test your camera settings while the light is still soft and even.

attractions.name: Yun Lai Viewpoint
attractions.description: Reached by car or scooter, this hilltop village complex offers tea service & panoramic valley views.

attractions.name: Santichon Village
attractions.description: Pony rides & a rustic Ferris wheel at a Chinese village, plus shops for artisanal souvenirs.

attractions.name: ศูนย์วัฒนธรรมจีนยูนนาน
attractions.description: This cultural center showcases the heritage of Yunnan Chinese immigrants who settled in Pai, with traditional architecture, exhibits of textiles, and artifacts from cross-border trade routes. The peaceful courtyard gardens and photo opportunities in period costume make it a charming glimpse into the region's multicultural roots.
attractions.local_name: ศูนย์วัฒนธรรมจีนยูนนาน
attractions.tip: Visit during weekday mornings when local volunteers are present to share oral histories about Yunnan migration stories that aren't covered in the written displays.

attractions.name: ทางขึ้นทะเลหมอกหยุนไหล
attractions.description: This steep trail leads to Yun Lai Viewpoint, where morning mist rolls through the valley between mountain peaks, creating ethereal cloud seas that submerge the landscape below. The sea of mist phenomenon transforms the viewpoint into one of Pai's most photographed natural spectacles.
attractions.local_name: ทางขึ้นทะเลหมอกหยุนไหล
attractions.tip: Arrive before 6:30 AM during cool season for the densest mist. The trail is steep and slippery when wet, so wear proper footwear and bring a flashlight if hiking in darkness.

attractions.name: Morpaeng Waterfall
attractions.description: Lush natural area featuring a quaint waterfall, multiple swimming pools & mountain views.

attractions.name: Doi Kiew Lom Viewpoint
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint overlooks rolling green mountains and the serpentine valleys that define Pai's rural landscape, with misty mornings revealing layers of ridges fading into the distance. The panoramic vista captures both sunrise and sunset angles, making it ideal for photographers seeking dramatic light over untouched highlands.
attractions.local_name: ดอยเขียวหล่ม
attractions.tip: Arrive 30 minutes before sunrise when mist blankets the valleys below and the viewpoint is nearly empty, avoiding tour groups that arrive mid-morning.

attractions.name: Wat Pa Tam Wua
attractions.description: This forest monastery and meditation retreat sits in a peaceful valley surrounded by hills, featuring cave shrines, a giant Buddha statue, and simple wooden pavilions where monks and visitors practice silent meditation. Come for the serene atmosphere and the chance to experience a day in the life of Buddhist monastic practice, with free basic accommodation and vegetarian meals available.
attractions.local_name: วัดป่าถ้ำวัว
attractions.tip: Visitors can stay overnight for free in basic dormitories and participate in morning alms-giving, meditation sessions, and Dhamma talks—bring modest clothing and respect the silence rules.

attractions.name: Kho Ku So Bamboo Bridge
attractions.description: This handwoven bamboo bridge stretches 800 meters across emerald rice paddies, rebuilt annually by local farmers using traditional techniques. Walk barefoot across the gently swaying structure at sunset when golden light bathes the surrounding fields and distant mountains.
attractions.local_name: สะพานไม้ไผ่คูโส๊ะ
attractions.tip: Visit during rice planting season (June-July) or harvest time (November-December) when the fields are most photogenic. Arrive before 5pm to cross before the bridge closes at dusk.

attractions.name: sunset spot
attractions.description: This elevated viewpoint near the walking street offers unobstructed views across Pai's valley as the sun dips behind distant mountain ridges, painting the sky in shades of amber and violet. The relaxed hillside setting with simple seating makes it the perfect wind-down spot after exploring town.
attractions.local_name: จุดชมพระอาทิตย์ตก
attractions.tip: Arrive 30 minutes before sunset to claim a good spot and watch the light gradually change across the valley—the pre-sunset glow is often more photogenic than the actual sunset.

attractions.name: Wat Nam Hu
attractions.description: Compact Buddhist temple with a fish pond, picturesque views & a tranquil atmosphere.

attractions.name: Mueang Paeng Hot Spring
attractions.description: Natural hot spring pools tucked in a riverside garden setting where locals soak their feet in mineral-rich waters that reach comfortable bathing temperatures. The peaceful atmosphere and genuinely therapeutic waters make it a relaxing break from Pai's busier attractions.
attractions.local_name: น้ำพุร้อนเมืองแปง
attractions.tip: Bring eggs to boil in the hottest spring pools while you soak—locals sell them at the entrance but you'll save money buying from town markets first.

food.name: Two Sisters Restaurant
food.where: 401 Tambon Mae Hi, Amphoe Pai, Chang Wat Mae Hong Son 58130, Thailand
food.tip: Try their northern Thai khao soi and fresh spring rolls. Best visited early evening before the tourist crowds arrive.
food.local_name: ร้านอาหารสองสาว

food.name: Centrico Pai Mexican Restaurant
food.where: 138 Chai Songkhram Rd, Tambon Wiang Tai, Amphoe Pai, Chang Wat Mae Hong Son 58130, Thailand
food.tip: Try their authentic carnitas tacos and fresh guacamole. Best visited during evening hours when the kitchen is fully stocked.
food.local_name: เซนทริโก ไป เม็กซิกัน เรสเตอรองค์

food.name: Pad Thai Pai Walking Street
food.where: 9C5V+HH8, Wiang Tai, Pai District, Mae Hong Son 58130, Thailand
food.tip: Order the boat noodles as a side—locals swear by the broth. Visit after 6pm when the street is most lively.
food.local_name: ผัดไทยไผ่ วอล์คกิ่งสตรีท

food.name: Khao Soi Pai
food.where: Pai, Pai District, Mae Hong Son 58130, Thailand
food.tip: Order the khao soi gai (chicken). Best visited for lunch when noodles are freshest. Located on Pai's main walking street.
food.local_name: ข้าวซอยไป

food.name: Thai food bar
food.where: 63/1, ตำบลเวียงใต้, Pai, Pai District, Mae Hong Son 58130, Thailand
food.tip: Order the northern Thai khao soi or larb during lunch for best freshness. Less crowded before 6pm on weekdays.
food.local_name: ร้านอาหารไทย

food.name: Jerk Spot Pai
food.where: 75, moo3, Pai, Amphoe Pai, Chang Wat Mae Hong Son 58130, Thailand
food.tip: Try the jerk chicken – marinated Caribbean-style with local Thai spices. Best visited during evening for authentic atmosphere.
food.local_name: เจิร์กสปอต ปาย

food.name: Na's Kitchen
food.where: 55 1, Tambon Wiang Tai, Amphoe Pai, Chang Wat Mae Hong Son 58130, Thailand
food.tip: Order the northern Thai curry dishes early morning when freshly made. Local favorite for authentic Pai home cooking.
food.local_name: ครัวนา

food.name: Khao soi jitlut
food.where: 85 Thetsaban 1 Rd, Pai, Amphoe Pai, Chang Wat Mae Hong Son 58130, Thailand
food.tip: Order the khao soi gai (chicken) early morning for the freshest broth. Their curry paste is made daily.
food.local_name: ข้าวซอยจิตรลัต

food.name: Sugarcane Home
food.where: 30M3 Pai Walking Street, ตำบล เวียงใต้ อำเภอปาย แม่ฮ่องสอน 58130, Thailand
